UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

PROCLAMATION
TO THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY:
WHEREAS, The budgetary needs of the several depart
ments, boards, commissions and agencies of the government of
Kentucky for the two years beginning July 1, 1936, have not yet
been determined by the General Assembly of the Common
wealth of Kentucky; and
WHEREAS, Such budgetry needs must be determined before
the amount of revenue to meet such needs can be ascertained:
NOW, THEREFORE, In order to determine the budgetary
needs of the several departments, boards, commissions and
agencies of Kentucky, and according to the power vested in me
by the Constitution of Kentucky to convene the General As
sembly into Extraordinary Session, I do issue this, my proclama
tion, convening the General Assembly of Kentucky in Extraor
dinary Session at the seat of government at Frankfort, Ken
tucky, at two o'clock P. M., on Monday, March 9, 1936, for the
sole purpose of considering the following subject:
(1) To consider, determine and prepare a budget which
will meet the needs of the several departments, boards, commis
sions and agencies of the government of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky, for a period of two years from July 1, 1936, and only
said subject.
You will therefore in accordance with this proclamation,
assemble in Extraordinary Session at the seat of government
at Frankfort, Kentucky, on the day and date above written, to
consider the subject herein mentioned and only said subject.
Given under my hand at 6:05 o'clock P. M., this 7th day of
March, 1936.
By the Governor: (Signed) A. B. CHANDLER,
Governor of Kentucky.
(Signed) CHAS. D. ARNETT,
Secretary of State.
[SEAL]

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE
GENERAL ASSEMBLY, WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE
CITY OF FRANKFORT, KENTUCKY, ON MONDAY, MARCH
THE NINTH, AND ENDED ON THURSDAY, MARCH THE
TWENTY-SIXTH, 1936.
CHAPTER 1.
AN ACT appropriating money for the operation, maintenance,
support, and functioning of the various officers, departments,
boards, commissions, institutions, agencies, and subdivisions
of the State government, of the Commnowealth of Kentucky,
for the fiscal years ending June 30, 1937, and June 30, 1938,
designating the funds and sources from which said appropria
tions are to be paid, describing the manner in which the
same are to be paid, providing for the payment into the State
treasury of all fees and other miscellaneous receipts collected
by all the different agencies of the State, providing for the
establishment of certain revolving funds, authorizing and em
powering the Governor of the Commonwealth to equitably
reduce, or adjust, the appropriations made to each agency, and
repealing all blanket and continuing appropriations not pro
vided for in this Act and all laws or parts of laws in c'onflct
with any provisions thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Section One (1). Executive Department. There
are hereby appropriated out of the General Expend
iture Fund for the Executive Department of the
State government, for the fiscal year ending June
30, 1937, the following sums for the following pur
poses :

2 Acts of the General Assembly
(a-one) (a-1) For salaries of the Governor, pri
vate secretary, stenographic help and other em
ployees, and for printing, stamps, office supplies,
extra help and other operations of the department,
the sum of $16,000.00.
(b-one) (b-1) For contingent and maintenance
fund, the sum of $6,000.00.
(c-one) (c-1) For salaries and other expenses of
the Executive Cabinet, the sum of $4,500.00.
There are hereby appropriated out of the Gen
eral Expenditure Fund for the Executive Depart
ment of the State government, for the fiscal year
ending June 30, 1938, the following sums for the
following purposes:
(a-two) (a-2) For salaries of the Governor, pri
vate secretary, stenographic help and other em
ployees, and for printing, stamps, office supplies,
extra help, and other operations of the department,
the sum of $16,000.00.
(b-two) (b-2) For the contingent and mainte
nance fund, the sum of $6,000.00.
(c-two) (c-2) For salaries and other expenses of
the Executive Cabinet, the sum of $4,500.00.
Section Two (2). Lieutenant Governor. There
is hereby appropriated out of the General Expendi
ture Fund for the use and benefit of the Lieutenant
Governor, for the fiscal year ending June 30, 1937,
for clerical hire and other expenses, the sum of
$500.00.
There is hereby appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund for the use and benefit of the
Lieutenant Governor, for the fiscal year ending
June 30, 1938, for clerical hire and other expenses,
the sum of $1,000.00.
Section Three (3). Department of State. There
is hereby appropriated out of the General Expendi

a clerk and other expenses of the Land Office.

ney General, the sum of $8,000.00.
There are hereby appropriated put of the Gen

collecting statistics, as is now, or may hereafter be,

which said department is now, or may hereafter be,

expenses, and all other necessary expenses of the
division, there is hereby appropriated from the Gen

All fees and operating receipts collected by the

to the credit of the General Expenditure Fund.
However, from such receipts so credited to the Gen

Law, not to exceed at any time the amount of re

Expenditure Fund. Withdrawals from the said re

(f-one) (f-1) For the use and benefit of the Ken

regardless of whether heretofore specified in this
Act, of every department, division, subdivision,

the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall be covered
into the State Treasury immediately after the close
of each month and placed to the credit of the Gen
eral Expenditure Fund, excepting those revenues
which are specifically allocated to the Highway De
partment. From these fees and receipts so covered

penditure Fund, the revolving fund accounts shall
be set up as provided in this Act.
Section Fifty-Seven (57). The appropriations
herein made for administration, operation, mainte
nance and/or capital outlay for any department,
institution, bureau, board, commission, or other
State agency, shall not be available for expenditure
until allotted as now provided by law, and are here
by declared to be maximum and conditional appro
priations, except where the Governor determines
an emergency exists in any department, institution,
bureau, board, commission, or other State agency
requiring an additional allotment of funds to be
made from the General Emergency Fund hereby
placed at his disposal in Section 44 of this Act. .
Since the actual needs of each department, insti
tution, bureau, board, commission, or other State
agency can only be anticipated and not finally deter
mined by the Legislature and, since the total amount

76 Acts of the General Assembly
of revenue accruing to the General Expenditure
Fund, from which these appropriations are hereby
made, can only be anticipated through existing laws,
and those that may hereafter be enacted, it is the
purpose and intention of the Legislature that the
State expenditures for each fiscal year covered in
this Act shall not exceed the total revenues accruing
to the General Expenditure Fund for the same fiscal
year. To carry out the intention and purpose of
the Legislature upon the determination of the con
ditions set forth, to wit: The needs of each depart
ment, institution, bureau, board, commission, or
other State agency and the total revenue accruing
to the General Expenditure Fund, which conditions
can not be finally determined until after the ad
journment of this session of the Legislature, the
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky is
hereby authorized, empowered, and directed to as
certain the needs of each department, institution,
bureau, board, commission, or other State agency,
and to ascertain the total revenues accruing to the
General Expenditure Fund of the Commonwealth
and, upon the ascertainment of said facts, he is
hereby authorized, empowered, and directed by the
Legislature to prevent an overdraft, or deficit, in
any fiscal year for which appropriations are herein
made by equitably reducing, without discrimination,
the appropriations herein made to any department,
institution, bureau, board, commission, or other
State agency; provided that the power hereby
granted to the Governor shall not permit any reduc
tion of the appropriation of any department, in
stitution, bureau, board, commission, or other State
agency that will actually impair the necessary gov
ernmental functions of any such department, insti
tution, bureau, board, commission, or other State
agency.

courts shall not affect or impair any of the remain

CHAPTER 2.
AN ACT appropriating money for the 1936 Special Sessions of
the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky,
and declaring an emergency to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
Section One (1). There is hereby appropriated
out of the general expenditure fund for the payment
of salaries and other necessary expenses of the 1936
Special Sessions of the General Assembly, the sum
of One Hundred Thousand Dollars ($100,000).
Section Two (2). Whereas, the funds heretofore
appropriated for the 1936 General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky are practically ex
hausted, and whereas, the work of the 1936 General
Assembly is not completed, an emergency is de
clared to exist and this Act shall become effective
upon its passage and approval by the Governor.
Approved March 26, 1936.

PROCLAMATION
TO THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY OP THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
WHEREAS, The anticipated revenues from existing tax
laws are inadequate to finance the several departments, boards,
commissions and agencies of the government of Kentucky for
the biennial period beginning July 1, 1936; and
WHEREAS, The State budget for said biennial period can
not be balanced without additional revenues; and
WHEREAS, An appropriation act has already been passed
by the General Assembly setting forth the financial needs of the
several departments, boards, commissions and agencies of the
State government; and
WHEREAS, The existing tax laws cannot reasonably be
expected to produce more than eleven million five hundred thou
sand dollars ($11,500,000.00) for each of the years of the biennial
period beginning July 1, 1936; and
NOW THEREFORE, In order to raise the necessary reve
nues to balance the State budget and meet the financial needs
of the several departments, boards, commissions and agencies
of the State of Kentucky, and according to the power vested in
me by the Constitution of Kentucky to convene the General
Assembly into Extraordinary Session, I do issue this, my procla
mation, convening the General Assembly of Kentucky, at two
o'clock p. m. on Monday, March 30, 1936, for the sole purpose
of considering the following subject:
(1) To pass laws raising the necessary revenues, in addi
tion to those anticipated from existing tax laws, to balance the
State budget and meet the financial needs of the several de
partments, boards, commissions and agencies of the government
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for a period of two years
beginning July 1, 1936, and only said subject.
You will therefore in accordance with this proclamation,
assemble in Extraordinary Session at the seat of government at
Frankfort, Kentucky, on the day and date above written, to con
sider the subject herein mentioned and only said subject.
Given under my hand at 3:25 o'clock p. m., this 27th day of
March, 1936.
By the Governor: A. B. CHANDLER
Governor of Kentucky
CHARLES D. ARNETT
Secretary of State

CHAPTER 1.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation on the sale and dis
tribution of alcoholic beverages, and declaring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Section One (1). Definitions. Whenever used
in this Act, unless the context requires otherwise:
(One) "Person" means and includes individ
uals, partnerships, joint stock companies, associa
tions, fiduciaries, and corporations.
(Two) "Alcohol" means and includes ethyl alco
hol hydrated oxide of ethyl or spirit of wine, from
whatever source or by whatever process produced.
(Three) "Alcoholic beverage" means and includes
alcoholic spirits, liquor, rum, wine, beer, and every
liquid or solid, patented or not, containing one per
cent or more of alcohol by volume and capable of
being consumed by a human being and every spu
rious or imitation liquor sold as, or under any name
commonly used for, alcoholic beverages, whether
containing any alcohol or not. Provided, that there
is excepted from this definition of alcoholic bever
ages the following: (a) medicinal preparations

2 Acts of the General Assembly
manufactured in accordance with formulas pre
scribed by the United States Pharmacopoeia, na
tional formulary or the American Institute of
Homeopathy that are unfit for use for beverage pur
poses; (b) patented, patent and proprietary med
icines that are unfit for use for beverage purposes;
(c) toilet, medicinal and antiseptic preparations
and solutions that are unfit for use for beverage
purposes; and (d) flavoring extracts and syrups
that are unfit for use for beverage purposes.
(Four) "Beer" means any fermented undis-
tilled alcoholic beverage of any name or description,
manufactured from malt wholly or in part, or from
any substitute therefor.
(Five) "Licensee" means any person to whom a
license has been granted by the State of Kentucky
or agency thereof to engage in the manufacture, dis
tribution, sale, transportation, or other traffic in
alcohol.
(Six) "Manufacturer" means and includes a
brewer, vinter, distiller, or rectifier and any other
person, whether included in the aforesaid categories
or not, engaged in the production and or bottling
of alcoholic beverages. "Bottling" means the
placing of alcoholic beverages in any retail con
tainer irrespective of the material from which said
container is made.
(Seven) "Wholesaler" means any one, whether
distributor, jobber, broker, agent, or other person,
whether enumerated in this Act or not, who dis
tributes alcoholic beverages for the purpose of being
sold at retail; provided, that after July one (1),
one thousand nine hundred thirty-six (1936), the
term wholesaler shall not be deemed to include any
subsidiary of a manufacturer or cooperative of re
tail outlet.

Chapter 1 3
(Eight) "Retail outlet" means and includes re
tailers, hotels, restaurants, railroad dining cars,
clubs, and any person who sells alcoholic beverages
to consumers or to any person other than a manu
facturer licensed under the laws of Kentucky or of
some other State or a wholesaler licensed under the
laws of Kentucky or of some other State or a retail
outlet licensed under the laws of Kentucky or of
some other State or a State or Commonwealth or a
State or Commonwealth liquor corporation.
(Nine) "Retail sale" means sale by a retail out
let.
(Ten) "Retail container" means and includes
any bottle, can, barrel, or other container which
without separable intermediate container holds alco
holic beverage and which is suitable and destined
for sale to a retail outlet, whether it is suitable for
delivery to the consumer or not.
(Eleven) "Spirits" means and includes whis
keys, brandies, distilled spirits, cordials, liquors,
alcohol, and any other alcoholic beverage, whether
or not included in the aforesaid categories, except
beer, and wine which contains less than twenty-four
(24) per cent alcohol by volume.
(Twelve) "Wine" means the product of the
normal alcoholic fermentation of the juices of fruits,
with the usual processes of manufacture and normal
additions, and includes champagne, sparkling, and
fortified wine of an alcoholic content not to exceed
twenty-four (24) per cent by volume. Provided,
however, that "wine" shall include preparations or
mixtures vended in retail containers if such prep
arations or mixtures contain not to exceed fifteen
per cent (15%) of alcohol by volume.
(Thirteen) "Department" means the Depart
ment of Revenue of Kentucky.

4 Acts of the General Assembly
Section Two (2). Bate of tax on beer. There is
hereby levied upon the sale or distribution by sale
or gift a tax of one dollar and fifty cents ($1.50) on
each barrel of thirty-one (31) gallons and a like or
proportional rate per gallon, or fractional part
thereof, on beer sold or distributed in any other
container of more or less thirty-one (31) gallons.
Section Three (3). Rate of tax on wine. There
is hereby levied upon the sale or distribution by
sale or gift a tax of twenty-five cents ($0.25) on
each gallon of wine, and a like or proportional rate
per gallon on wine sold or distributed in any other
container of more or less than one gallon; provided
that in no event may the tax be less than five cents
($0.05) on the sale or distribution of any retail con
tainer of wine. Provided, however, that the provi
sions of this Act shall not apply to the manufacture,
sale, gift or distribution of any wine manufactured,
sold, given away or distributed and used solely for
Sacramental purposes.
Section Four (4). Rate of tax on spirits. There is
hereby levied upon the sale or distribution by sale
or gift a tax of one dollar and four cents ($1.04) on
each wine gallon of spirits and a like or proportional
rate per gallon on spirits sold or distributed in any
other container of more or less than one gallon.
Nothing herein contained shall affect or be deemed
to affect or to repeal, amend, or modify any tax im
posed by any provisions of chapter one hundred
forty-nine (149) of the Acts of the General Assem
bly of one thousand nine hundred thirty-four (1934),
except section three (3) (section four thousand two
hundred fourteen (4214) a-fourteen (14), Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes, one thousand nine hundred
thirty-four (1934 Supplement) thereof which is
hereby repealed.
Section Five (5). Who shall pay the tax. The

Chapter 1 5
wholesaler who purchases or otherwise acquires
title to any alcoholic beverage made taxable by this
Act shall within twenty-fouii (24) hours from the
time possession of said beverage is acquired affix or
cause to be affixed the stamps required under the
provisions of this act, providing that in the event a
wholesaler in purchasing or otherwise acquiring
title to said beverage resells or otherwise disposes
of same without ever having actual possession of
said bever'age shall affix or cause to be affixed said
stamps as provided herein before delivery of said
beverage shall be made to the purchaser or donee
of said beverage.
Section Six (6). Importer shall pay tax. The
Wholesaler who imports any alcoholic beverage in
any retail container for eventual retail sale or' dis
tribution by gift or retail sale in this State shall pay
the tax imposed in this Act and affix the stamps as
provided herein upon such beverage before the same
shall be sold or offered for sale or distribution by
gift or sale; provided that said tax shall be paid
and said stamps shall be affixed in every instance
within twenty-four (24) hours after receipt of said
beverage by the importer thereof.
Section Seven (7). Failure to pay tax. In the
event that the licensee or any other person who
places any alcoholic beverage in any retail con
tainer, or who imports such beverage in any retail
container, fails to pay the tax imposed in this Act
when due, the purchaser thereof and each subse
quent purchaser or other recipient in turn becomes
liable for the tax. Provided, that this section shall
not relieve the preceding licensee from liability for
the tax and for any penalties prescribed in this Act
for violation thereof.
Section Eight (8). Purchase of alcoholic bever
age by manufacturer or wholesaler. No manufac

6 Acts of tSe General Assembly
turer of alcoholic beverages shall sell or distribute
by gift or sale any such beverages except to another
manufacturer or to a wholesaler. No wholesaler
shall sell or distribute by gift or sale any alcoholic
beverages except to another wholesaler or to a retail
outlet, provided however that manufacturers of beer
may sell to retail outlets or may sell to consumers
for personal use and not for resale.
Section Nine (9). Purchase of alcoholic bever
age by retail outlet. No retail outlet shall purchase
or have in possession any alcoholic beverage, other
than beer purchased from a Kentucky manufac
turer, which shall have been purchased other than a
licensed Kentucky wholesaler not a manufacturer.
No common carrier or other person shall transport
into the State any alcoholic beverage unless said
beverage shall be consigned to a licensee other than
a retail outlet. Provided, that the proprietor of a
licensed railroad dining car may purchase for sale
in such car alcoholic beverages from wholesalers
outside the State when the tax imposed by this Act
on such beverages shall have been paid before entry
into the State.
Section Ten (10). Stamps evidence of payment of
tax. The person who pays the tax on any alcoholic
beverage shall evidence the payment by the pur
chase and affixing of stamps issued by the Depart
ment, which stamps he shall place upon each retail
container in such a manner as the Department may
prescribe. Provided, however, that notwithstand
ing section five (5) of this Act, the Commissioner of
Revenue, in the case of beer, may order that per
sons selling to retailers affix said stamps to invoices.
Provided, further, that said retailers shall keep all
such stamped invoices for a period of two years for
examination by the Commissioner or his authorized
agent.

Chapter i 7
Section Eleven (11). Who shall sell stamps. The
Commissioner of Revenue shall prescribe, prepare
or cause to be prepared, and have available for sale,
stamps of such denomination, kinds, and quantities
as he shall deem necessary for the payment of taxes
imposed by this Act.
Section Twelve (12). Out-of-state vendor may
purchase stamps. Vendors of alcoholic beverages
without the State, licensed to sell such beverages by
their own states and by the Federal government,
may purchase stamps from the Commissioner of
Revenue of Kentucky and affix them in the manner
prescribed by said Commissioner to retail contain
ers of alcoholic beverages to be sold to wholesalers
in Kentucky for eventual retail sale or distribution
by gift or retail sale in this State, or to proprietor
of licensed railroad dining cars ; in which event the
importer of such alcoholic beverages within the
State shall not be required to pay the tax on such
beverages nor to affix the stamps.
Section Thirteen (13). Prohibition of sale with
out stamps. No alcoholic beverage in retail con
tainers shall be sold for eventual retail sale or dis
tribution by gift or retail sale in the State unless
a stamp evidencing payment of the tax on said alco
holic beverages is in place on such container. No
common carrier or other person shall transport al
coholic beverages in retail containers from one point
to another within the State unless such stamp is in
place. No retail outlet shall purchase or have in
possession any alcoholic beverage except in retail
containers to which such stamps have been properly
affixed.
Section Fourteen (14). Sales by licensees when
ultimate destination is undetermined. The sale or
distribution of alcoholic beverages manufactured in
or imported into this State for shipment permanent

8 Acts of the General Assembly
ly out of the State to be sold through retail outlets
without the State and consumed without the State
shall not be subject to the tax imposed by this Act.
Provided, however, that the containers of such alco
holic beverages for shipment permanently out of the
State to be sold through retail outlets without the
State and consumed without the State shall have
affixed thereon, in such manner as the Department
may prescribe, a stamp provided under chapter one
hundred forty-nine (149), Acts of the General As
sembly of one thousand nine hundred thirty-four
(1934) or (if no such stamp is required) an export
stamp which must be furnished by the Department
without charge.
Section Fifteen (15). Only one tax required.
Nothing in this Act shall be construed to require
the payment of the tax imposed herein on the sale
of the same alcoholic beverages more than once.
The Commissioner of Revenue is empowered to is
sue stamps without charge to any licensee when
necessary to give effect to this section.
Section Sixteen (16). Cancellation of stamps.
It shall be the duty of the person, whether retailer,
consumer, or other, who opens each retail container
of alcohol beverage, to deface and cancel all tax
stamps on such container at the time it is first
opened. Provided, that any retailer shall leave all
such cancelled stamps in plain view unless the con
tainer is empty of alcoholic beverage.
Section Seventeen (17). Stamps affixed to bar
rels. The licensee who sells any alcoholic beverage
destined to be sold to any retail outlet in barrels or
other containers which are not ordinarily suitable
for delivery to the consumer shall pay the tax herein
imposed on the sale of the beverage before selling
or offering it for sale and shall affix the stamps in

Chapter 1 9
the manner prescribed by regulation, unless such
tax has already been paid and such stamps affixed.
Section Eighteen (18). Possession of unstamped
alcoholic beverage. The possession of unstamped
alcoholic beverage, except as otherwise explicitly
provided herein, shall be deemed prima facie evi
dence that such beverage is possessed with the in
tent to sell or distribute the same contrary to law.
Section Nineteen (19). Keport of licensee. For
the purpose of assisting in the enforcement of this
Act, every licensee, whether subject to the payment
of taxes imposed by this Act or not, on or before the
tenth day of each month, shall render to the Com
missioner of Revenue a sworn or affirmed statement
in writing of all of his trafficking in alcohol during
the preceding month. Such statement shall be taken
directly from the books of such licensee and shall
set forth, on forms furnished by the Commissioner,
the quantities, kinds, and destinations of alcoholic
beverages purchased, held, and sold ; the tax stamps
purchased, affixed, and on hand ; and such additional
information as the Commissioner may require.
Such statement shall include alcohol destined for
industrial and other non-beverage purposes, and
alcoholic beverages destined for sale outside the
State, as well as alcoholic beverages subject to the
tax imposed by this Act. Every person who shall
import any alcoholic beverage in Kentucky for the
purpose of sale or distribution by gift or sale in
this State or any other State is hereby required to
transmit to the Commissioner of Revenue within
twenty-four (24) hours after receipt of same and
original copy of each invoice for such beverage.
Section Twenty (20). Powers of the Department
of Revenue. The administration of this Act is
vested in the Department of Revenue, which may
prescribe forms and promulgate rules and regula

10 Acts of the General Assembly
Hons needed for the enforcement of this Act. The
Department, through a representative appointed by
it in writing, is hereby authorized to examine the
books, records, papers, files, equipment and/or in
ventory of any person made liable for any tax under
the provisions of this Act; to examine witnesses
under oath for purposes appropriate for enforce
ment of this Act; and to examine the books, rec
ords, papers, files, equipment and/or inventory of
manufacturers, wholesalers, retailers, or carriers
for the purpose of determining whether the Act is
being violated. The Department shall have the
power to institute legal proceedings by the Attorney
General of Kentucky, or any attorney duly author
ized by him, for the purpose of ascertaining the
amount of the tax due and/or enforcing the collec
tion thereof with penalties and interest thereon, and
for the purpose of enjoining the operation of the
business of the delinquent. The Department may at
or after the commencement of an action for the
purpose of collecting the amount of tax due and
penalty and interest thereon, as set out herein, have
an attachment against the property of the person
liable for tax or garnishment of his creditors with
out the execution of bond on the filing of an affidavit
showing the nature of the claim, that it is just, the
sum which the Department believes the Common
wealth should recover, on any of the grounds pro
vided by the Civil Code of Practice for the issuance
of an attachment in civil causes.
Section Twenty-One (21). Commissioner of
Revenue to Require Information. The Commis
sioner of Revenue shall reguire common carriers to
provide information in such form as he may deem
wise respecting all shipments of alcoholic beverages
to, from, or between persons in Kentucky.
Section Twenty-Two (22). Violation of this Act.

Chapter 1 11
(a) In addition to the remedies herein granted the
Commonwealth for such violations, any person who
shall violate any of the provisions of this Act shall
also be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and pun
ished by a fine of not less than fifty dollars ($50.00)
nor more than one thousand dollars ($1,000.00), or
by imprisonment of not less than thirty (30) days
nor more than one (1) year, or by both such fine and
imprisonment. Provided, that in case such violator
be a corporation, joint stock company, association
or fiduciary, then the principal officer and/or the
officer or officers responsible for such violation may
be punished by such imprisonment, (b) Any per
son required under this Act to pay any tax herein
imposed who shall fail to pay the tax due in the
time specified in this Act shall pay a penalty equal
to twenty per cent (20%) of the amount of the tax
due and delinquent together with interest at six per
cent (6%) per annum. In addition to all other pen
alties herein imposed, the Department of Revenue
shall report such delinquency to the Department of
Business Regulation, which may revoke the license
of such person, (c) If any person shall forge,
counterfeit, or alter any stamp, receipt or writing
provided for by any provision of this Act with in
tent to defraud the Commonwealth, or if he shall
be concerned in printing, writing, signing, uttering,
or passing any such forged and counterfeit papers
(whether passed separately or attached to a retail
container) as above described, knowing the same to
be forged and counterfeited, he shall be confined in
the penitentiary not less than two (2) nor more than
ten (10) years.
Section Twenty-Three (23). Tax on sale of in
ventories on hand. As to stocks of alcoholic bev
erages in the hands of a wholesaler or retail outlet
and acquired before but on hand when the taxes

12 Acts of the Gexeral Assembly
imposed by the previous sections of this Act be
comes effective, there is hereby imposed for the first
sale or distribution by gift or sale thereof after
such effective date of the aforementioned taxes, a
tax equal in amount to that imposed by the previous

Section Twenty-Four (24). Disposition of reve
nue. All revenues collected under the provisions of
this Act shall be covered through the Department

salers of beer are hereby authorized to deduct from
the amount of the tax collected under the provisions
of this act three (3) per cent of the said amount to
cover expenses incident to the reporting and re
mitting of such taxes as provided in this Act-
Section Twenty-Five (25). Emergency. Where
as the present State revenue does not meet the im
mediate needs of the maintenance of the State gov
ernment, its agencies and subdivisions, an emer
gency is declared to exist and this Act shall become
a law and be effective upon its passage and ap
proval by the Governor. Be it provided, however,
that pending preparation of stamps and develop
ment of other administrative plans, the effective
date of Sections two (2), three (3), four (4), and

(Chapter 2
twenty-three (23) shall be one o'clock A. M., of the
date three calendar weeks from the day this meas
ure is approved by the Governor.
Approved on 9th day of April, 1936.
CHAPTER 2.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation for the sale of vinous
and spirituous liquors repealing, amending and re-enacting
the last paragraph of Section six (6), Article IV, Chapter one
hundred and forty-six (146), Acts of the General Assembly
of one thousand nine hundred thirty-four (1934), codified as
Section two thousand five hundred fifty-four (2554) b-sixteen
(16), Baldwin's Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes,
and declaring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One (1). There is hereby imposed a tax
of seven cents (7c) upon the sale or distribution by
gift or sale of spirituous liquors in containers of
half pint and less in size, except as hereinafter pro
vided.
Section Two (2). The last paragraph of Section
six (6), Article IV, Chapter one hundred and forty-
six (146), of the Acts of the General Assembly of
one thousand nine hundred thirty-four (1934), is
hereby repealed, amended and re-enacted so that
when re-enacted, it shall read as follows : "All such
sales shall be in unbroken package in either quart,
one fifth gallon, pint, and half -pint sizes, and shall
not be consumed on the premises except in the case
of spirituous liquors which are contained in two-
ounce (2 oz.) or less containers, that said containers
shall not be sold by any licensed dispensary unless
such dispensary has obtained a license to sell spirit
uous liquor by the drink. That the tax levied upon
spirituous liquor contained in two ounce (2 oz.) con
tainers or containers less in size shall be two cents
(2c) upon each such container of spirituous liquor."

revenue does not meet the immediate needs for the

and approval by the Governor. Be it provided,
however, that pending preparation of stamps and

Shall proclaim. Approved May 1, 1936.
CHAPTER 3.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation and declaring an
emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One (1). (a) "Bottled drinks" for pur
poses of this Act shall be denned as all beverages
sold in sealed, capped or stopped bottles, except
those having an alcoholic content in excess of one
per cent (1%) by volume, and include beverages
such as coca cola, ginger ale, lime soda, pepsicola,
fruit juices, grapeola, soda water, and other bever
ages whether or not similar to those enumerated in
this paragraph, under whatever trade name vended.
Beverages described in this paragraph are defined
for purposes of this Act as "soft drinks" whether
sold in bottles or not.
(b) The word "syrup" shall be defined for pur
poses of this Act as being the compound mixture or
basic ingredients used in the making, mixing or com
pounding of soft drinks, by mixing the same with
water, ice, fruits, milk, and/or other commodities
whether or not similar to those enumerated here,

Chapter 3 15
to make a vendable soft drink, commonly known as
a "fountain drink," among such syrups being coca
cola syrup, cherocola syrup, lemon syrup, vanilla
syrup, chocolate syrup, rock candy syrup, simple
syrup, grapeola syrup, cherry smash syrup, and/or
prepared syrups, whether or not similar to those
enumerated here, under whatever name vended,
manufactured or sold for the purpose of making soft
drinks, other than bottled drinks. "Syrups" shall
not include any commodity containing in excess of
one per cent (1%) alcohol by volume.
(c) The term "sale" shall be defined for pur
poses of this Act as including all transfers and ex
changes of tangible personal property for money or
other valuable consideration. The term "sale" also
includes gifts.
(d) The term "distributor" shall be defined for
purposes of this Act as including every person
charged under the Act with the duty of paying the
tax or taxes herein imposed.
(e) The word "person" shall be defined for pur
poses of this Act as including individuals, firms,
joint stock companies, syndicates and corporations.
(f) The term "retailer" is defined for purposes
of this Act as one engaged as a merchant in selling
personal property not intended for resale. The
word "retail" and forms thereof shall be inter
preted in keeping with this definition.
(g) The term "jobber" is defined for purposes
of this Act as one not a manufacturer who sells
personal property intended for resale.
(h) The term "manufacturer" is defined for pur
poses of this Act in the usual sense of a person who
fabricates or compounds commodities in such way
as to add to their value. It includes those who op
erate as bottlers.

id Acts of the General Assembly
(i) The word "candy" is defined for purposes
of this Act as including all those commodities com
mercially so designated and also such additional
confections as are composed wholly or in part of
candy. The term does not include cakes or cookies
or other sweetened breads which may be filled,
coated or iced with confections similar to candy.
(j) The term "ice cream" is defined for purposes
of this Act as all frozen or semi-frozen products of
milk, fruit, fruit juices and/or other commodities
commonly called ice cream, sherbert, ice, frozen
puddings and mousse. The term does not include
confections herein defined as candy.
(k) "Chewing gum" is defined for purposes of
this Act as all preparations of chicle and/or other
substances sold and/or used under the name of
chewing gum.
(1) The word "cosmetics" is defined for purposes
of this Act as including any preparation manu
factured, sold, or distributed for use as a facial or
body powder, lotion or cream; rouge, lipstick, nail
polish or covering, nail polish remover, cuticle cream
or oil; hair tonics, pomades, ointments, oils, dyes,
rinses, or shampoos; eyebrow and eyelash pencils,
and eyebrow, eyelash or eyelid coloring; bath salts
and every variety of perfumes and toilet waters,
astringents; mouth washes, tooth pastes and pow
ders, depilatories, deodorants; and any and all
other preparations whether or not enumerated in
this section and whether or not similiar to those
enumerated, which are advertised, sold or used for
the purpose of softening, cleansing, or beautifying
the skin, hair, teeth or nails. Provided, however, that
neither prescriptions prepared by physicians for a
specified individual and filled by licensed pharma
cists nor soaps retailing at thirty cents (30e) a

Chapter 3 17
pound or less shall be deemed to be cosmetics for the
purposes of this Act.
Section Two (2). (a) A state tax is hereby im
posed on the sale or use of commodities enumerated
in this section in the amounts specified as follows:
Bottled drinks, one cent (1c) for each five cents
(5c) or fractional part thereof of the retail selling
price.
The tax imposed upon the sale of candies and
chewing gum shall be collected by the retailer sell
ing such candies and/or chewing gum and shall be
accounted for by him to the Department of Revenue
and he shall make a monthly report covering such
sales on or before the 10th of each month following
the month during which this Act becomes effective
and shall accompany said report with a certified or
cashier's check for the amount of the tax.
The tax imposed upon the sale of ice cream shall
be collected by the person making the first sale of
ice cream in the Commonwealth of Kentucky and
such person shall account for same to the Depart
ment of Revenue and he shall make a monthly re
port covering such sales on or before the 10th of
each month following the month during which this
Act becomes effective and shall accompany said re
port with a certified or cashier's check for the
amount of the tax.
The tax herein imposed upon the receipt of cos
metics in this state by any retailer shall be accounted
for by him to the Department of Revenue and he
shall make a monthly report covering such receipts
on or before the 10th of each month following the
month during which this Act becomes effective and
shall accompany said report with a certified or
cashier's check for the amount of the tax.
The Department of Revenue, in lieu of the ad

18 Acts of the General Assembly
ministrative plan hereinabove set out, may require
the affixing of stamps evidencing payment of the
taxes on the sale, use and/or receipt of candies,
chewing gum, ice cream and/or cosmetics at such
a time or times and in such a manner and to such
packages or types of packages as it may deem de
sirable.
Syrup, seventy-six cents (76c) a gallon.
(b) There is also hereby levied and shall be as
sessed and collected on the sale of soft drinks manu
factured or sold within the State, which drinks are
manufactured without the use of any syrup upon
which the tax has previously been paid and which
are other than bottled drinks, a tax of one cent (1c)
for each five cents (5c) or fractional part thereof of
the retail price of such drinks. This tax shall be
collected in accordance with rules and regulations
promulgated by the Department of Revenue for the
purpose of rendering this statute effective ; provided
that when it is impractical to use stamps and crowns
the Department of Revenue may dispense with the
use of stamps and crowns and may require the dis
tributor of such drinks to make returns in writing in
such form as the Department of Revenue may pre
scribe and as frequently as the said Department may
require. In such event the Department of Revenue
may require payment of the tax at the time the re
turn is submitted or, at its discretion, may require
the taxpayer to pay all or a part of the tax after the
return has been audited and the tax newly assessed
by the Department of Revenue. Provided that the
tax shall not be deemed in any case to apply to milk,
coffee, and/or tea, when dispensed as such and un
mixed with other ingredients.
(c) A state tax is hereby imposed on the sale of
all candies of twenty per cent (20%) of the retail

Chapter 3 19
selling price. Provided, however, that the Depart
ment of Revenue is hereby authorized to assume a
retail mark-up equal to that which is customary in
the trade.
(d) A state tax is hereby imposed on the sale of
ice cream of seven cents (7c) a quart and alike or
proportionate rate on more or less than one (1)
quart. The first sale of ice cream in the Common
wealth made by a distributor domiciled in Kentucky
shall be subject to the tax and each subsequent sale
shall be tax free if the said ice cream shall be ac
companied by an invoice on which it is clearly indi
cated that the tax herein imposed has been assumed
by the said distributor.
(e) A state tax is hereby imposed on the sale of
chewing gum of one cent (1c) a five cent (5c) pack
age or fractional part thereof.
(f) A state tax is hereby imposed on the receipt
of cosmetics in the State by any Kentucky retailer
equal to twenty per cent (20%) of the invoice price
plus transportation cost, if any, to the Kentucky
retailer.
(g) A state tax is hereby imposed on the sale of
all nuts of twenty per cent (20%) of the retail sell
ing price. Provided, however, that the Department
of Revenue is hereby authorized to assume a retail
mark-up equal to that which is customary in the
trade.
Two (2). In addition to the taxes herein imposed,
each person in this State engaged in the business
of selling and/or receiving commodities on the sale
and/or receipt of which a tax is herein imposed,
who is not required by any other law of the State of
Kentucky to be licensed and pay a franchise tax, is
hereby required to procure a license from the De
partment and pay to the Treasury of the Common

20 Acts of the General Assembly
wealth, an annual franchise tax of two dollars
($2.00).
Section Three (3). The tax imposed herein upon
the sale of bottled drinks and for syrups, when sold
in bottles, shall be paid by affixing stamps in the
manner and at the times herein set forth, according
to rules and regulations which may be promulgated
by the Department of Revenue to make this Act
effective.
Section Four (4). All syrups shall be stamped
within twenty-four hours of the time such syrups
are manufactured, packed, or imported into the
state, and before the same shall be sold or offered
for sale, by affixing to each individual barrel, box,
or other container, whether or not enumerated here,
an adhesive stamp in such denomination as to show
the payment of the tax levied herein. The stamp
shall be so affixed that the box, bottle or other con
tainer can not be opened by the ordinary process
without breaking or tearing such stamp or cutting
or destroying said barrel, box, bottle, or other con
tainer, and it shall be so affixed as to require the
continued application of steam or water to remove
it without opening the said barrel, box, bottle or
other container.
Section Five (5). All bottled drinks manufac
tured, bottled, or imported for sale in this state,
shall be stamped within twenty-four (24) hours of
the time such drinks are manufactured, bottled, or
imported and before the said drinks are sold or
offered for sale, by affixing to each individual bottle
either a crown or cap provided by the Department
of Revenue for the purpose or an adhesive stamp
in such denomination as to denote payment of the
tax herein levied. If an adhesive stamp is used, it
shall be so affixed that it will be broken when the

Chapter 3 21
bottle is opened or unsealed ; and it shall be so affixed
as to require the continued application of steam or
water to remove it without opening the bottle.
Be it provided, however, that the person required
by Sections 4 and 5 of this Act to affix adhesive
stamps and/or crowns or caps to such containers
shall be allowed a discount of three per cent (3%)
of the amount of the tax to cover breakage and
leakage.
Section Six (6). (a) In the event adhesive
stamps shall be attached to individual bottles of
soft drinks, no cancellation or obliteration of the
stamp shall be required, but tax stamps affixed to
containers of syrups to be used at soda fountains,
or otherwise for compounding soft drinks, shall be
cancelled in the following manner, to wit: By the
distributor's writing or stamping, or causing to be
written or stamped, with ink or indelible pencil
across said stamp said distributor's initials or
name and the date upon which the stamp was affixed.
(b) Whoever makes use of any adhesive stamp
to denote any tax imposed by this Act without can
celling such stamp, if required herein, shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor, and, if convicted, shall pay
a fine of not more than one hundred dollars ($100.00)
or be imprisoned for not more than thirty (30) days
for each offense.
Section Seven (7). (a) The administration of
this Act is vested in the Department of Bevenue,
which may prescribe forms and promulgate rules
and regulations needed for the enforcement of the
Act. The Department of Bevenue or any represen
tative appointed by it in writing, is hereby author
ized to examine the books, records, papers, files,
equipment and/or inventory of any distributor per
taining to sales and/or receipts made taxable by

22 Acts of the General Assembly
this Act, to examine witnesses under oath for the
purpose of verifying returns made by distributors
or for other purposes involving enforcement of this
Act, and to examine the books, records, papers, files,
equipment and/or inventory of manufacturers, job
bers, retailers, or carriers for the purpose of deter
mining whether the Act is being violated.
(b) The Department of Revenue shall have pow
er to institute legal proceedings for the purpose of
ascertaining the amount of tax due and of enforcing
the collection thereof with penalties thereon and for
the purpose of enjoining the operation of the busi
ness of the distributor until said tax is paid.
(c) The Department of Eevenue may at or after
the commencement of an action for the purpose of
collecting the amount of tax due and penalty and
interest thereon, as set out herein, have an attach
ment against the property of the distributor or gar
nishment of his debtors without the execution of
bond, upon filing an affidavit showing the nature of
the claim, that it is just, the sum which the Depart
ment of Revenue believes the state should recover
from the distributor, and any of the grounds upon
which an attachment is authorized by the civil code
of practice in civil causes.
(d) Any information obtained by the Depart
ment of Revenue or any agent or representative
thereof, as a result of the reports, investigations or
verifications, herein authorized and required to be
made, shall be confidential as respecting the busi
ness of any distributors and any person divulging
such information, except on the order of a court of
competent jurisdiction, or to an officer of the Com
monwealth entitled to receive the same in his official
capacity, shall be fined not exceeding five hundred
dollars ($500.00), provided, however, that this pro
vision shall not prevent the Department of Revenue

Chapter 3 23
from publishing statistics of tax receipts and vol
umes of sales in such a manner as not to reveal in
formation concerning the business of any individual
distributor.
(e) The Department of Revenue is hereby au
thorized and directed to prescribe the forms of ad
hesive stamps and caps or crowns herein required
to be used by distributors. The Department shall
have adhesive stamps and crowns carrying the de
sign prescribed, prepared for sale to distributors
in such a manner as it may deem proper. The ad
hesive stamps shall be sold at their face value, the
crowns or caps shall be provided to distributors at
the cost of manufacture plus transportation charges
plus stamp tax value.
Be it provided, however, that the Department in
its discretion may permit said distributor, on the
furnishing of a bond deemed good and sufficient by
the Department in an amount in no event less than
twice the average monthly tax paid by such dis
tributor with a corporate surety authorized to do
business in Kentucky, to purchase such adhesive
stamps and/or crowns or caps on a credit of not
more than thirty (30) days. .
(f) The methods of breaking packages and the
methods of affixing stamps that shall be employed
by distributors under the terms of this Act shall be
prescribed by the Department of Revenue so as to
render effective the enforcement of this Act.
(g) The Department of Revenue shall keep a
suitable record of the sale of all stamps and crowns
which shall show the dates of sale thereof and the
names of the purchasers.
Section Eight (8). Whoever removes, washes,
restores, alters, or otherwise prepares an adhesive
stamp or crown with intent to use, or cause to be

24 Acts of the General Assembly
used, after it has already been once used, or who
ever knowingly or willfully buys, sells, offers for
sale, or gives away such washed, restored, or al
tered stamp or crown, to any person, or knowingly
uses the same, or has in his possession any washed,
restored or altered stamps or crowns which have
been removed from commodities to which they have
been previously affixed, or whoever, for the purpose
of indicating the payment of any tax herein im
posed, uses again any stamp or crown that has
theretofore been used, for the purpose of denoting
the payment of the tax provided in this Act, or who
ever prepares, buys, sells or offers for sale, or know
ingly has in his possession any counterfeit stamps
or crowns, is guilty of a misdemeanor and, upon con
viction, shall be punished by a fine of not more than
one thousand dollars ($1,000.00) or by imprison
ment for not more than one (1) year or by both
methods.
Section Nine (9). Any person required by this
Act to affix the stamps or use official crowns, as pro
vided in this Act, who shall fail properly to affix
the stamps or crowns, within the time prescribed
by law, shall be required to pay as a part of the tax
imposed hereunder a penalty of one hundred per
cent (100%) of the amount of tax due to be assessed
and collected by the Department of Revenue as other
taxes are collected. Any commodities in the place
of business of any person required by the provisions
of this Act to stamp the same shall be prima facie
evidence that such articles are intended for sale or
use in this State.
Section Ten (10). Any manufacturer liable to
pay the tax imposed by this section, who has in his
inventory commodities made taxable hereunder,
may defer payment of the tax to such time as may
be approved by the Department of Revenue, not be

Chapter 3 25
yond the time of sale, upon making bond in favor
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in an amount
equal to the tax on the maximum unstamped inven
tory (said bond to be approved by the Department
of Revenue) to insure the Commonwealth against
loss of tax revenue due to deferring the stamping of
the commodities as required hereunder. It is the
intent and purpose of this section to render it rea
sonably possible for manufacturers to stamp the
bottles or other containers in which all beverages,
the sale of which is taxable hereunder, shall be
found in the hands of retailers.
Section Eleven (11). No person shall sell, give
or lend any stamps or crowns, nor shall any person
purchase, accept, receive, or borrow any stamps or
crowns from any person ; it being the intent hereof,
in order to prevent fraud and insure collection of
the taxes due, to limit all sales and transfers of such
stamps or crowns to the Department of Revenue or
its authorized representative.
Section Twelve (12). It is the intent and pur
pose of this Act to impose the tax levied herein once
only upon the sales of the same commodities made
taxable hereunder. Any commodity, on account of
the sale of which the tax has been paid, shall not
occasion payment of a second or other tax regard
less of the number of times the commodity may be
sold in this State.
Section Thirteen (13). Every distributor is
hereby required to preserve written records of sales
and/or receipts made taxable by this Act in such
form as the Department of Revenue may prescribe,
to keep invoices of commodities purchases, which
are taxable hereunder, and to maintain such other
records and submit such reports as the Department
may require for the enforcement of this Act.

26 Acts of the General Assembly
Section Fourteen (14). No suit shall be main
tained in any court to restrain or delay the collec
tion or payment of the tax levied by this Act. The
aggrieved taxpayer shall pay under protest the tax
as and when required and may at any time within
two years from the date of such payment sue the
Commonwealth through its agent, the Auditor of
Public Accounts, in an action at law in any court,
state or federal, otherwise having jurisdiction of
the parties and subject matter, for the recovery of
the tax paid with legal interest thereon from the
date of payment. If it is finally determined that
said tax or any part thereof was wrongfully col
lected, for any reason, it shall be the duty of the
Auditor of Public Accounts to issue his warrant on
the Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
for the amount of such tax so adjudged to have been
wrongfully collected together with legal interest
thereon. The Treasurer shall pay the same at once
out of the General Expenditure Fund of the State
in preference to other warrants or claims against
the Commonwealth. A separate suit need not be
filed for each individual payment made by any tax
payer, but a recovery may be had in one suit for as
many payments as may have been made.
Section Fifteen (15). If commodities, the sale of
which is made taxable by this Act, are distributed
under such circumstances that the Constitution and
laws of the United States or of Kentucky preclude
the possibilities of imposing the tax, then such tax
shall not apply. In case of sales made into another
state, the Department of Revenue may dispense
with stamps or crowns or may require stamps on
cases or may make appropriate regulations pre
scribing some other means whereby the tax may not
be made payable.

Chapter 3 27
Section Sixteen (16). Any person who shall
violate any of the provisions of this Act for which
specific criminal penalty is not herein provided,
shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and upon
conviction shall be punished by a fine of not more
than five hundred dollars ($500.00) for each offense
and said person's license shall be subject to revoca
tion at the discretion of the court. In the event
that any such person shall be convicted of a second
offense, such offender, in addition to other penal
ties herein provided, shall forfeit for such time as
the Court may determine, the right to purchase any
stamps from the Department of Revenue and shall
have said person's license revoked for the pre
scribed period. The penalty involving the revoca
tion of licnse may be applied also in those instances
in which a specific penalty is herein provided; said
revocation of its license to be in addition to the spe
cific penalty prescribed in this Act.
Section Seventeen (17). All revenue derived
from the operation of this Act as collected and re
corded shall be deposited through the Department
of Finance into the State Treasury, and credited to
the General Expenditure Fund.
Section Eighteen (18). The provisions of this
Act are severable. If any provision, section, or
paragraph thereof, or if the application of any pro
vision, section or paragraph to any class of sales or
uses affected by the operation of the Act, should be
held unconstitutional or invalid, such decision re
garding the provision, section, paragraph, or appli
cation shall not affect or impair the remainder of
the Act, it being hereby declared the legislative in
tent to tax separately the sale of each class of prop
erty contemplated by any of its provisions.
Section Nineteen (19). Whereas, the present
Acts— 2

28 Acts of the General Assemble
State revenue does not meet the immediate needs
of the maintenance of the State Government, its
agencies and subdivisions, an emergency is declared
to exist and this Act shall become a law and be
effective upon its passage and approval by the Gov
ernor. Be it provided, however, that pending prep
aration of stamps and crowns and the creation of
the necessary administrative set-up, the effective
date of Section 2 of this Act shall be one o'clock
