CHAPTER 4.
AN ACT concerning revenue and taxation and declaring an
emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Section One (1). Definitions: Whenever used in
this Act unless the context requires otherwise:
(a) "Manufacturer" shall mean and include
any person engaged in the manufacture or pro
duction of cigarettes;
(b) "Wholesaler" shall mean and include any
person who sells at wholesale or who distributes cig
arettes for the purpose of being sold at retail. The
term includes any manufacturer, distributor, jobber,
broker, agent, or other person, whether or not enum
erated in this section, who so sells or so distributes ;
(c) "Retailer" shall mean and include any per
son who sells to a consumer or to any person for
any purpose other than resale;
(d) "Department" shall mean and include the
Department of Revenue of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky ;

Chapter 4 29
(e) "Person" shall mean and include any indi
vidual, firm, partnership, association, corporation,
or other group or combination acting as a unit.
Section Two (2). There is hereby imposed on
the sale of cigarettes made of tobacco or any sub
stitute therefor one cent (1c) on each ten cents (10c)
or fractional part thereof of the retail selling price.
Section Three (3). The taxes imposed by this
Act shall be paid by affixing stamps in the manner
and at the time herein set forth. The stamps shall
be affixed to each individual package of cigarettes
by the manufacturer of such cigarettes when and as
sold, if such cigarettes are manufactured within the
State of Kentucky, otherwise they shall be so
stamped by wholesalers within seventy- two (72)
hours after such cigarettes are received by them.
If received by retailers, they shall be stamped within
twenty-four (24) hours after their receipt by him.
All cigarettes must be stamped before being sold
in this State.
Section Four (4). The stamps required herein
to be affixed to the box or container in which, or
from which, cigarettes are normally sold to retailers
shall be so affixed that the boxes or containers can
not be opened without breaking the stamp; and it
shall be so affixed as to require the continued appli
cation of steam or water to remove without opening
the said box or container.
Section Five (5). The stamps provided for in
this Act shall be purchased from the Department
and from no one else. Said Department is empow
ered to prescribe rules and regulations governing
the sale and distribution of said stamps.
Section Six (6). Where any cigarettes for the sale
of which a tax is herein imposed are manufactured
for shipment and sale without the State, the said

30 Acts of the General Assembly
manufacturer shall affix thereon, in the same man
ner as the other stamps herein provided for are re
quired to be affixed, an export stamp to be furnished
without cost by said Department. In the case of
any cigarettes upon which any export stamps herein
provided for be sold and shipped to a regular dealer
in such article in another state for sale outside the
state, the seller in this state shall be entitled to a
refund of the actual amount of tax paid, upon con
dition that he shall make an affidavit that the goods
were so sold and shipped, and shall furnish from
purchaser written acknowledgment that he has re
ceived such goods and as part of said acknowledg
ment the amount of stamps thereon. The refund
may be in the form of stamps of sufficient value to
cover said refund or if the seller shall so desire the
Department shall certify to the Auditor of Public
Accounts the refund to which said seller is entitled,
and the Auditor shall draw his order in said amount
upon the State Treasury, which shall pay the said
refund to said distributor.
Section Seven (7). Whoever removes, washes,
restores, alters, or otherwise prepares any of the
stamps herein mentioned with intent to use or cause
the same to be used after it has already been used ;
or whoever counterfeits such stamps or knowingly
or willfully sells, offers for sale, or gives away any
such counterfeit, washed, restored, or altered stamp
to any person; or knowingly uses the same or has
in his possession any washed, restored, or altered
stamps which have been removed from the article
to which they have been previously affixed, or any
counterfeit stamp; or whoever, for the purpose of
indicating the payment of tax hereunder, uses any
stamp that has heretofore been used for the purpose
of denoting the tax provided in this Act; or pre
pares, buys, sells or offers for sale, or knowingly

Chapter 4 31
has for sale, in his possession any counterfeit stamp
shall be' guilty of a misdemeanor and upon his con
viction shall be punished by a fine of not less than
one hundred dollars ($100) nor more than one thou
sand dollars ($1,000) or by imprisonment for one to
five years, or both.
Section Eight (8). Any person required by this
Act to affix the stamps as provided by this Act who
shall fail properly to affix the stamps within the
time prescribed by law, shall be required to pay as
part of the tax imposed herein, one hundred per cent
(100%) of the amount of the tax due to be assessed
and collected by the Department as other taxes are
collected, and such tax and penalty thereon shall
bear interest at the rate of six per cent (6%) per
annum.
Any article required by this Act to bear a stamp
found in the place of business of any person en
gaged in selling cigarettes shall be prima facie evi
dence that such articles are intended for sale.
Section Nine (9). The administration of this Act
is vested in the Department which may prescribe
rules and regulations needed for the enforcement of
this Act. The Department, or any representative
appointed by it in writing, is hereby authorized to
examine the books, records, papers, files, equipment,
and/or inventories of any manufacturer, wholesaler,
or retailer, pertaining to sales made taxable by this
Act; to examine witnesses under oath with the pur
pose of verifying returns made by those required
by this Act to make returns, or for other purposes
involving the enforcement of this Act ; and to exam
ine the books, records, papers, files, equipment, and/
or inventories of manufacturers, wholesalers, deal
ers, or carriers for the purpose of verifying the ac
curacy of the reports under this Act or dealing with

32 Acts of the General Assembly
this Act. If this Act is being violated the Depart
ment shall have power to institute legal proceedings
for the purpose of regulating and enforcing the col
lection therefor, with interest and penalties thereon.
The Department may for the purpose of collecting
the amount of the tax due with interest thereon have
an attachment against the property of the one from
whom the tax, penalty, and interest is due or gar
nishment of his creditors without execution of bond
upon the filing of an affidavit alleging the nature of
the claim, that it is just, the sum which the Depart
ment believes the Commonwealth should recover
from the defendant, and any grounds upon which
the Civil Code authorizes the issuance of an attach
ment in civil causes.
Any information obtained by the Department or
any agent or representative thereof as a result of
the reports or investigations herein required to be
made shall be confidential as respects the individual
business. Any person divulging the information,
except on order of a court of competent jurisdiction
or to an officer of the Commonwealth entitled to re
ceive the same in his official capacity, shall be fined
a sum not exceeding five hundred dollars ($500),
provided, that this provision shall not prevent pub
lishing statistics of tax receipts and sales if such
publication reveals information as concerning the
individual business of any manufacturer, whole
saler, or retailer.
Section Ten (10). The Department is hereby
authorized and directed to prescribe the forms of
the stamps herein provided for. It shall have such
stamps prepared for sale in such manner as it may
deem proper. Such stamps shall be sold at their face
value. The methods of breaking packages and con
tainers and the methods of affixing the stamps shall

Chapter 4 33
be prescribed by the Department in such way as to
enforce the provisions of this measure.
Section Eleven (11). It is the intent or purpose
of this Act to impose the tax herein levied one time
upon the sale of cigarettes. Any cigarettes on ac
count of which a tax has been paid shall not occasion
payment of a second or other tax regardless of the
number of times the cigarettes may be sold within
this State.
Section Twelve (12). Every manufacturer, whole
saler, and retailer, is hereby required to preserve
written records of sales made taxable by this Act,
to keep invoices of commodities received by him,
and to maintain other records and submit such ad
ditional reports as the Department may require for
the enforcement of this Act.
Section Thirteen (13). No suit shall be main
tained in any court to enjoin or delay the collection
of the tax herein imposed on any grounds whatever.
The aggrieved party shall pay the tax under protest
as and when due, and may at any time within two
years from the date of such payment sue the Com
monwealth of Kentucky through its agent, the Audi
tor of Public Accounts, in an action in any court,
state or federal, otherwise having jurisdiction of
the parties, and subject matter for recovery of the
tax so paid with interest thereon from the date of
payment. If it is finally determined that said tax
or any part thereof was wrongfully collected for
any reason, it shall be the duty of the Auditor of
Public Accounts to issue his warrant on the Treas
ury of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the
amount of such tax so adjudged to have been wrong
fully collected together with interest thereon, and
the Treasurer shall pay the same at once out of the
General Expenditure Fund of the State in prefer
ence to other warrants or claims against the Com

34 Acts of the General Assembly
monwealth. Separate suits need not be filed for each
separate payment made, but recovery may be had
in one suit for all sums paid which are not barred
by the aforesaid limitation of two years.
Section Fourteen (14). When any cigarettes, the
sale of which is made taxable by this Act, are sold
or distributed under such circumstances that the
Constitution and laws of the United States preclude
imposition of the tax, then such tax shall not apply.
Section Fifteen (15). Any manufacturer, whole
saler, or retailer who shall fail or refuse to make
any return required by the Department of him or
shall refuse to permit the Department, its authorized
agents or representatives to examine the books,
records, papers, files, and/or equipment of such per
son pertaining to the business made taxable by this
Act, or makes an incomplete, false, or fraudulent
return hereunder or who attempts to do anything
hereunder to evade full disclosure of the facts or to
avoid the payment in whole or in part of the tax or
interest theeron due shall be deemed guilty of a mis
demeanor and upon conviction shall be fined not ex
ceeding one thousand dollars ($1,000) and in case
the offender is an individual may bo imprisoned in
the county jail not exceeding six (6) months, or both.
Such fines shall be in addition to other fines and
penalties hereunder prescribed.
All the revenue derived from the operation of this
Act shall as collected be covered into the State
Treasury and credited to the General Expenditure
Fund.
Section Sixteen (16). The imposition of the taxes
herein provided shall not become effective until one
(1) o'clock, A. M., three calendar weeks from the
date this Act is approved by the Governor. Within
twenty-four (24) hours thereafter, and before any

Chapter 5 35
sale is consummated wholesalers and retailers shall
affix the stamps required by this Act on all ciga
rettes on hand and acquired before the effective date
of the imposition of the taxes herein provided.
Section Seventeen (17). Whereas, the present
state revenue does not meet the immediate needs
for the maintenance and support of the state govern
ment, an emergency is declared to exist; except as
otherwise herein provided, and this Act shall become
a law effective upon its passage and approval by
the Governor.
Approved May 1, 1936.
CHAPTER 5.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation of certain motor
fuels and declaring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Section One (1). Definitions: Whenever used in
this Act, unless the context requires otherwise,
(a) "Motor vehicles" shall mean and include all
vehicles, engines, machines or mechanical con
trivances which are propelled by internal combus
tion engines or motors;
(b) "Fuels" shall mean and include all' com
bustible gases and liquids used in an internal com
bustion engine for the generation of power to propel
vehicles of any kind or character on the public high
ways except such fuels as are subject to the tax im
posed by House Bill No. 41, Special Revenue Ses
sion of 1936;
(c) "Public Highways" shall mean and include
every way or place, of whatever nature, generally
open to the use of the public as a matter of right
for the purposes of vehicular travel and notwith

36 Acts of the General Assembly
standing that the same may be temporarily closed
for the purpose of construction, reconstruction,
maintenance or repair;
(d) "Person" shall mean and include any indi
vidual, association, firm, co-partnership, corpora
tion, receiver, trustee, conservator or other officer
appointed by any State or Federal Court ;
(e) "Use" shall mean and include, in addition
to its original meaning, the receipt of fuel by any
person into a motor vehicle or into a receptacle from
which fuel is supplied by such person to his own or
other motor vehicles ;
(f) "User " shall mean any person who uses or
consumes fuel within this State in an internal com
bustion engine for the generation of power to
propel vehicles of any kind or character on the
public highways of this State ;
(g) " Duly licensed user ' ' shall mean and include
any user holding an unrevoked license issued by this
State ;
(h) "Department" shall mean the Department
of Revenue of the Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Section Two (2). Levy of tax and exemptions.
An excise tax of five cents (5c) per gallon is hereby
imposed on the use of such fuel by any person
within this State only when such fuel is used in an
internal combustion engine for the generation of
power to propel motor vehicles of any kind or char
acter on the public highways of Kentucky to be com
puted in the manner hereinafter in this Act set
forth and paid by the users. Provided, however,
that no tax is hereby imposed on the use of any fuel
if the Constitution of the United States or of this
State precludes such tax.
Section Three (3). Allocation of tax. All taxes
collected under the provisions of Section 2 of this

Chapter 6 37
Act shall bo covered into the State treasury and
shall be used only to defray the expenses of the ad
ministration of this Act and for the purpose of con
struction, reconstruction, maintenance and repair
of roads, highways and bridges.
Section Four (4). Application for license: Con
tents: Licensing of users,
A. It shall be unlawful for any user to use or
consume any fuel within this State unless such user
is the holder of an uncancelled license issued by the
Department. To procure such license, every used
shall file with the Department an application under
oath and in such form as the Department may pre
scribe setting forth the name and address of the
user.
B. Concurrently with the filing of an application
for a license, every user shall file with the Depart
ment a bond of the character stipulated and in the
amount provided for in Section 5 of this Act. No
license shall be issued upon any application unless
accompanied by such a bond.
C. In the event that —
(1) Any application for a license to use fuel as
a user in this State shall be filed by any person
whose license shall at any time theretofore have
been cancelled for cause by the Department, or in
case
(2) Said Department shall be of the opinion that
such application is not filed in good faith or that
(3) Such application is filed by some person as a
subterfuge for the real person in interest whose
license or registration shall theretofore have been
cancelled for cause by said Department.
Then and in any of said events the Department
after a hearing of which the applicant shall have
been given five (5) days notice in writing and in
which said applicant shall have the right to appear

38 Acts of the General Assembly
in person or by council and present testimony, shall
have and is hereby given the right and authority to
refuse to issue to said person a license certificate
in this State.
D. Upon the filing of the application for a license,
a filing fee of One Dollar ($1.00) shall be paid to
the Department.
E. The application in proper form having been
accepted for filing, the bond having been accepted
and approved and the other conditions and require
ments of this section having been complied with,
the Department shall issue to such user a license
certificate and such license shall remain in full force
and effect until cancelled as provided in this Act.
F. The license certificate so issued by the Depart
ment shall not be assignable and shall be valid only
for the user in whose name issued and shall be dis
played conspicuously by the user.
G-. The Department shall keep and file all appli
cations and bonds with an alphabetical index
thereof, together with a record of all licensed users.
Section Five (5). Bond required of licensed
users.
A. Every user shall file with the Department a
bond
(1) in the minimum amount of $100 and maximum
amount of $10,000 on a form to be approved by the
Department and
(2) with surety approved by the Department as
surety thereon and
(3) upon which such user shall be the principal
obligor and this State shall be the obligee,
(4) conditioned upon the prompt filing of true re
ports and the payment by such user to the Depart
ment of any and all fuel excise taxes which are now
or which are hereafter levied or imposed by this
State, together with any and all penalties and/or

Chapter 5 39
interest thereon and generally upon faithful com
pliance with the provisions of this Act.
B. In the event that the liability upon the bond
thus filed by the user with the Department shall be
discharged or reduced, whether by judgment ren
dered, payment made, or otherwise, or if in the
opinion of the Department any surety on the bond
theretofore given shall have become unsatisfactory
or unacceptable, then the Department may require
the user to file a new bond with satisfactory sureties
in the same form and amount, failing which the De
partment shall forthwith cancel the license certifi
cate of said user. If such new bond shall be fur
nished by such user as above provided, the Depart
ment shall cancel and surrender the bond of said
user for which such new bond shall be substituted.
C. In the event that upon hearing, of which the
user shall be given five (5) days' notice in writing,
the Department shall decide that the amount of the
existing bond is insufficient to insure payment to
this State of the amount of the tax and any penal
ties and interest for which said user is or may at
any time become liable, then the user shall forth
with upon the written demand of the Department
file an additional bond in the same manner and form
with surety thereon approved by the Department
in any amount determined by the Department to be
necessary to secure at all times the payment by such
user to this State of all taxes, penalties, and inter
est due under the provisions of this Act, failing
which, the Department shall forthwith cancel the
license certificate of said user.
D. Any surety on any bond furnished by any
user as heretofore provided shall be released and
discharged from any and all liability to this State
accruing on such bond after the expiration of sixty
(60) days from the date upon which surety shall

40 Acts of the General Assembly
have lodged with the Department a written request
to be released and discharged. Provided, however,
that such a request shall not operate to relieve, re
lease or discharge such surety from, any liability
already accrued or which shall accrue, before the
expiration of said sixty (60) day period. The De
partment shall promptly, on receipt of notice of
such request, notify the user to furnish such bond
and unless such user shall, on or before the expira
tion of such sixty (60) day period, file with the
Department a new bond with surety satisfactory to
the Department in the amount and form hereinbe
fore in this section provided, the Department shall
forthwith cancel the license of said user. If such
new bond shall be furnished by said user as above
provided, the Department shall cancel and surren
der the bond of said user for which such new bond
shall be substituted.
Section Six (6). Tax reports.: Computation and
payment of tax.
A. For the pui-pose of determining the amount
of tax herein imposed, each user shall, not later
than the fifteenth (15) day of each calendar month,
file with the Department on forms prescribed by
said Department, monthly reports sworn to by the
user which shall include the total number of gallons
of fuels used within the State during the next pre
ceding calendar month.
B. The tax at the rate of five cents (5c) per gal
lon of fuel shall be computed on the number of gal
lons reported used within the State and shall con
stitute the amount of fuel tax payable for the next
preceding calendar month. Reports required in
subsection A of this section shall be accompanied
by certified or cashier's check, payable to the State
Treasurer of Kentucky for the amount of tax due
for such preceding calendar month.

Chapter 5 41
Section Seven (7). Potver of department to can
cel licenses: Surrender of bond.
A. If a user shall at any time file a false monthly
report of the data or information required of this
Act, or shall fail, refuse or neglect to file the
monthly report required by this Act or to pay the
full amount of the tax as required by this Act, the
Department may forthwith cancel the license of
said user and notify such user in writing of such
cancellation by registered mail to the last known
address of such user appearing on the files of the
Department.
B. Upon receipt of a written request from any
user licensed under this Act to cancel the license
issued to such user, the Department shall have the
power to cancel such license effective sixty (60)
days from the date of such written request, but no
such license shall be cancelled upon the request of
any user until and unless the user shall, prior to
the date of such cancellation, have paid to this State
all excise taxes payable under the laws of this State,
together with any and all penalties, interest and
fines accruing under any of the provisions of this
Act and until and unless the user shall have sur
rendered to the Department the license certificate
therefore issued to such user. If upon investiga
tion, the Department shall ascertain and find that
any person to whom a license has been issued under
this Act is no longer engaged in the use of fuel and
has not been so engaged for a period of six (6)
months, the Department shall have the power to
cancel such license by giving such person sixty (60)
days notice of such cancellation mailed to the last
known address of such person, in which event the
license certificate theretofore issued to such person
shall be surrendered to the Department.
C. In the event that the license of any user shall

42 Acts of the General Assembly
be cancelled by the Department as hereinbefore in
this section provided and in the further event that
said user shall have paid to this State all excise
taxes due and payable by it under this Act, together
with any and all penalties accruing under any of
the provisions of this Act, then the Department
shall cancel and surrender the bond theretofore
filed by said user.
Section Eight (8). Failure to report and pay
tax, penalties provided.
If any user neglects or refuses to make said re
turn, or pay the tax at the time hereinbefore pro
vided, the amount thereof, with an addition of a
penalty of twenty per cent (20%) thereof, with in
terest at the rate of six per cent (6%) per annum
from the date when clue until paid, shall be paid on
an account settled by the Department, as other taxes
are paid, settled and collected; and if collected by
proceedings in any court, an additional penalty of
twenty per cent (20%) shall be collected as is au
thorized to be collected under Section 4263 of Car
roll's Kentucky Statutes, 1930 edition, as amended
by House Bill No. One, First Extraordinary Ses
sion of 1936.
The said twenty per cent (20%) penalty thereon
and interest due shall be paid into the State treas
ury as part of the tax, and the additional twenty
per cent (20%) penalty collected by suit shall be
distributed as provided in Section 4263 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes, 1930 edition, as amended by
House Bill No. One, First Extraordinary Session
of 1936.
Section Nine (9). Department may estimate fuel
used.
A. Whenever any user shall neglect or refuse to
make and file any report for any calendar month as
required by this Act, or shall file an incorrect or

Chapter 5 43
fraudulent report, the Department shall determine,
after an investigation the number of gallons of fuel
with respect to which the user has incurred liability
under the fuel tax laws of this State, for any par
ticular month or months and assess and collect the
amount of taxes and penalties payable by the user
under this Act accordingly.
B. In any action or proceeding for the collec
tion of the fuel tax and/or any penalties or interest
imposed in connection therewith, an assessment by
the Department of the amount of the tax due and/or
the interest or penalties due to the State shall con
stitute prima facie evidence of the claim of the
State, and the burden of proof shall be upon the
user to show the assessment was incorrect and con
trary to law.
Section Ten (10). Reports from carriers trans
porting fuel.
A. Every railroad company, every street, subur
ban or inter-urban railroad company, every pipe
line company, every water transportation company,
and every common carrier transporting fuel, either
in interstate or in intra-state commerce, to points
within the State, and every person transporting fuel
by whatever manner to a point in this State from
any point outside of said State and with the definite
knowledge that such fuel is to be used in an internal
combustion engine for the generation of power to
propel a motor vehicle of any kind or character
upon the public highways of this State, shall report
under oath to the Department on forms prescribed
by said Department of all such deliveries of fuel so
made to points within this State.
B. Such reports
(1) shall cover monthly periods,
(2) shall be submitted within fifteen (15) days
after the close of the month covered by the report,

44 Acts of the General Assembly
(3) shall show the name and address of the per
son to whom the delivery of such fuel has actually
and in fact been made and such other reasonable
information deemed necessary by the Department.
Section Eleven (11). Retention of records by
users.
A. Each user shall maintain and keep for a pe
riod of two (2) years, such record or records of
fuel used within this State by such user, together
with invoices, bills of lading, and other pertinent
records and papers as may be required by the De
partment for the administration of this Act.
B. Penalty: Any person willfully violating any
of the provisions of this section shall be guilty of a
misdemeanor and shall, upon conviction thereof, be
sentenced to pay a fine of not less than One Dollar
($1.00) and not more than One Thousand Dollars
($1,000.00), and costs of prosecution, or to undergo
imprisonment for not more than one year, or both
in the discretion of the court.
Section Twelve (12). Powers of the Department
of Revenue.
A. The administration of this Act is vested in
the Department of Revenue, which may prescribe
forms and promulgate rules and regulations needed
for the enforcement of this Act.
B. The Department, through a representative
appointed by it in writing, is hereby authorized to
examine the books, records, papers, files, equipment
and/or inventory of any person made liable for any
tax under the provisions of this Act ; to examine wit
nesses under oath for purposes appropriate for en
forcement of this Act; and to examine the books,
records, papers, files, equipment and/or inventory
of manufacturers, wholesalers, retailers, or carriers
for the purpose of determining whether the Act is
being violated.

Chapter 5 45
C. The Department shall have the power to in
stitute legal proceedings by the Attorney General
of Kentucky, or any attorney duly authorized by
him, for the purpose of ascertaining the amount of
tax due and/or enforcing the collection thereof with
penalties and interest thereon, and for the purpose
of enjoining the operation of the business of the
delinquent.
D. The Department may at or after the com
mencement of an action for the purpose of collect
ing the amount of tax due and penalty and interest
thereon, as set out herein, have an attachment
against the property of the person liable for tax or
garnishment of his debtors without the execution of
bond on the filing of an affidavit showing the nature
of the claim, that it is just, the sum which the De
partment believes the Commonwealth should re
cover, and any of the grounds provided by the Civil
Code of Practices for the issuance of an attachment
in civil causes.
Section Thirteen (13). Information privileged.
Any information obtained by the Department or
any agent or representative thereof as a result of
a report, investigation, or verification herein author
ized to be made, shall be confidential and any person
divulging any such information except upon order
of a court of competent jurisdiction or to an officer
of the Commonwealth entitled to receive the same
in his official capacity shall be fined not less than
twenty-five dollars ($25.00) nor more than five hun
dred dollars ($500.00).
Section Fourteen (14). Restraining suits.
No suit shall be maintained in any court to re
strain or delay the collection of the tax herein im
posed on any grounds whatever but the aggrieved
taxpayer shall pay under protest the tax as and
when due and may at any time within two (2) years

46 Acts of the General Assembly
after such payment sue the Commonwealth through
its agent, the Auditor of Public Accounts, in any
action in any court, State or Federal, otherwise
having jurisdiction of the subject matter for the
recovery of the tax so paid with legal interest
thereon from the date of payment. If it can be
finally determined that said tax or any part thereof
was wrongfully collected for any reason it shall be
the duty of the Auditor of Public Accounts to issue
his warrant on the Treasurer of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky for the amount of such tax so adjudged
to have been wrongfully collected, together with
interest thereon; and the Treasurer shall pay the
same at once out of the Road Fund of the State in
preference to other warrants or claims against the
Commonwealth.
Separate suits need not be filed for each separate
payment by any taxpayer but recovery may be had
in one for all suits heretofore paid or not barred
by the limitation of two (2) years herein imposed.
Section Fifteen (15). Penalties for failure to
make returns, to pay tax, or to permit examination
of records.
Any person who shall fail, neglect or refuse to
make the returns and pay the tax herein prescribed,
or shall refuse to permit the Department, its assist
ants, agents or representatives appointed by it . in
writing, to examine the books, records, papers, files
and equipment of such person pertaining to the use
of fuel made taxable by this Act, or who makes any
incomplete, false or fraudulent return hereunder,
or who does, or attempts to do, anything whatever
to avoid a full disclosure of the amount of fuel used
or to avoid the payment of the whole or any part
of the tax or penalties due, or who shall fail to keep
and preserve records or make reports as provided
in this Act, shall be guilty of a misdemeanor, and

Chapter 5 47
upon conviction, shall be fined not less than twenty-
five dollars ($25.00) nor more than one thousand
dollars ($1,000.00), and in the case the offender is
an individual, he may be imprisoned in the county
jail not exceeding six (6) months, or both so fined
and imprisoned. Provided, that in case such violator
be other than a natural person then the principal
officer and/or the officer or officers responsible for
such violation, may be punished by such imprison
ment.
Section Sixteen (16). Discontinuance as a license-
user.
A. Whenever a user ceases to engage in business
as a user of fuel within this State it shall be the
duty of such user to notify the Department in writ
ing within fifteen (15) days after discontinuance.
All taxes, penalties and interest under this Act not
yet due and payable under the provisions of this
Act shall, together with any and all interest accru
ing or penalties imposed under this Act, notwith
standing any provisions thereof, become due and
payable concurrently with such discontinuance. It
shall be the duty of said user to make a report and
pay all such taxes, interest and penalties and to
surrender to the Department the license certificate
theretofore issued to said user by the Department.
B. Any person violating any of the provisions of
this section shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and
shall upon conviction thereof be sentenced to pay a
fine of not less than fifty dollars ($50.00) nor more
than three hundred dollars ($300.00) and costs of
the prosecution or to undergo imprisonment for not
more than one (1) year or both.
Section Seventeen (17). Exchange of informa
tion with other states.
The Department shall, upon request duly received,
from the officials to whom are intrusted the enforce

48 Acts of the General Assembly
ment of the fuel tax laws of any other state or the
federal Government, forward to such officials any
information which it may have in its possession
relative to the manufacturer, receipt, sale, use,
transportation and/or shipment by any person of
fuel. Provided, however, this section shall not apply
unless such state or Federal Government extend to
this State a similar privilege.
Section Eighteen (18). Separability clause.
The provisions of this Act are severable, and if
any of its provisions or any penalty provided by
this Act shall be held unconstitutional, such pro
vision or the particular penalty held to be invalid
shall not affect or impair the remainder of the Act
or such part of the penalties as are not held to be
invalid and the decisions of the courts shall not
affect or impair the remaining provisions of this Act
or any of the other penalties anywhere in this Act
provided. It is hereby declared as a legislative in
tent that this Act would have been adopted had any
unconstitutional provisions of such invalid penalties
not been included herein.
Section Nineteen (19). Emergency.
Whereas, the present State revenue does not
meet the immediate needs for maintenance of the
State government an emergency is declared to exist
and this Act shall become a law and be effective
upon its passage and approval by the Governor.
Neither approved nor disapproved.
CHAPTER 6.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation of gasoline and re
pealing certain sections and declaring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
Section One (1). Chapter one hundred and fifty,

Chapter 6 49
(150), of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1932,
now codified in Baldwin's Supplement to Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes, Sections 4224b-1 to 4224b-12, in
clusive, is hereby repealed.
Section Two (2). Definitions. Whenever used
in this Act unless the context requires otherwise :
(a) "Person" shall mean and include any indi
vidual or individuals, association, firm, co-partner
ship, corporation, receiver, trustee, conservator, or
other officer appointed by any State or Federal
court.
(b) "Gasoline" as used in this Act shall include
all liquid fuels, including liquids ordinarily, prac
tically and commercially usable in internal combus
tion engines for the generation of power, and all
distillates of, and condensates from petroleum, nat
ural gas, coal, coal tar, vegetable ferments and all
other products, so usable except kerosene, cleaner
solvent, fuel oil, diesel fuel and crude oil.
(c) "Dealer" is hereby defined as any person
who imports or causes to be imported in the State
of Kentucky gasoline as herein defined for use, dis
tribution, or sale and delivery in the State of Ken
tucky and after the same shall reach the State of
Kentucky; also any person who produces, refines,
manufactures or compounds gasoline in the State
of Kentucky for use, distribution or sale and deliv
ery in this State; also any person who receives gaso
line by tank car, barge or pipe line delivery from
any point within this State upon which gasoline the
excise tax levied by this Act has not been paid ; also
any person importing or causing to be imported into
this State, gasoline in the fuel tank or any auxiliary
tank of his motor vehicle in a quantity in excess of
thirty gallons.
(d) "Department" shall mean the Department
of Revenue of the Commonwealth of Kentucky.

50 Acts of the General Assembly
(e) Gasoline shall be deemed as "received"
when so designated by the term "received" in this
Act when it shall have been caused to be imported
into the State of Kentucky by any person for use,
distribution, or delivery and sale and when it shall
have been placed in tanks or other containers for
use or subject to withdrawal for use, distribution or
sale and delivery. Gasoline produced, refined, man
ufactured or compounded at any refinery in the
State of Kentucky by any person shall be deemed
to be "received" as the word "received" is used in
this Act by such person when the same shall have
been loaded into tank cars for delivery within the
State of Kentucky or placed in any tank from which
sales or deliveries are made at the refinery where
the same is produced, refined, manufactured or com
pounded. Gasoline imported by any person into
the State of Kentucky and received through deliv
ery by boat at a marine terminal for storage, or
delivered by pipe line at a pipe line terminal for
storage, shall be deemed to have been "received"
when the same shall have been loaded into tank cars
for delivery within the State of Kentucky or placed
in any tank from which sales or deliveries are made
at such respective marine or pipe line terminals.
Provided that dealers may sell gasoline to licensed
bonded dealers in the State of Kentucky in trans
port truck load, carload or cargo lots withdrawing
such gasoline from refinery, marine or pipe line ter
minal storage tanks, without paying the tax on such
gasoline. In such instances the licensed bonded
dealers purchasing such gasoline shall be deemed as
having "received" such gasoline, and shall assume
responsibility to the State for the payment of the
tax thereon and shall report and pay the tax on such
gasoline purchased and received.
(f) "Refinery" shall mean any place where gas

Chapter 6 51
oline is refined, manufactured, compounded or oth
erwise prepared for use.
(g) "Accountable losses" of gasoline shall be
construed to mean that "received" gasoline which,
through wrecking of transportation conveyance, ex
plosion, and/or fire, shall have been lost or de
stroyed; report of such loss made to the Depart
ment in a manner and form to be prescribed by the
Department and supported by positive and conclu
sive evidence in the judgment of the Department, of
such alleged loss, and confirmed in writing to the
reporting dealer by the Department after any inves
tigation deemed necessary by the Department to
establish the bona fide claim of such loss.
Section Three (3). Rate of tax on gasoline. An
excise tax of five cents (5c) per gallon is hereby im
posed on all gasoline received in this Commonwealth
as the word "received" is hereinbefore defined. No
other excise or license tax shall be levied or assessed
on gasoline by the State or any political subdivision
of the State. The tax hereby provided for shall be
paid by the dealer receiving such gasoline to the
State Treasurer in the manner and within the time
hereinafter specified. Nothing herein contained
shall be construed as authorizing or requiring the
collection of such tax upon any gasoline after the
same shall have been already once taxed under the
provisions of this Act.
Section Four (4). Allocation of tax from gaso
line. All taxes collected under the provisions of
Section 2 of this Act shall be covered into the State
Treasury and shall be used only to defray the ex
pense of the administration of this Act and for the
purpose of the construction, reconstruction, main
tenance and repair of roads, highways and bridges.
Section Five (5). Records required. Every
dealer receiving gasoline in the State of Kentucky

52 Acts of the General Assembly
shall keep and preserve for a period of two years
an accurate record of all such receipts as well as of
all production, refining, manufacture, compounding,
use, sale, distribution, and delivery of gasoline to
gether with invoices, bills of lading and other perti
nent records and papers as may be required by the
Department. Every person purchasing gasoline
from a dealer for the purpose of resale shall main
tain and keep for a period of one year a record of
all such gasoline so purchased and sold or used, the
amount of tax paid to the dealers as part of the pur
chase price together with delivery tickets, invoices,
bills of lading and such other records as the Depart
ment shall require.
Section Six (6). Reports required of refiners
and importers. Any person who or which produces,
refines, manufactures or compounds gasoline in the
State of Kentucky shall on or before the 10th day
of each month file a report with the Department on
forms prescribed for the purpose and covering the
next preceding calendar month of the number of
gallons of gasoline produced, refined, manufactured
or compounded, the number of gallons withdrawn
from refinery storage and shipped to points outside
of the State, the number of gallons withdrawn from
refinery storage and shipped to points within the
State of Kentucky upon which tax has not been
paid, and giving in detail the following data regard
ing each separate shipment ; date of shipment, num
ber of gallons in each shipment, name of owner and
license number of truck, if shipped by transport
truck, initial and number of tank car if shipped by
rail, name and owner of barge if shipped by water,
name and address of person to whom shipped, point
of shipment, point of destination and name of car
rier to whom delivered for transportation to desti
nation. Any person who or which imports and

Chapter 6 53
stores gasoline in any marine or pipe line terminal

Section Seven (7). Reports of transportation.
Every railroad or other transportation company
and every other person, firm, association, partner
ship or corporation transporting gasoline as defined
in Section 2 of this Act by whatsoever manner from
without the State to points within this State, or be
tween points within the State shall report to the
Department, on forms prescribed by the Depart
ment, showing the name and address of each per
son, firm or corporation to whom deliveries of gas
oline have been made, the name and address of the
original consignee, if deliveries are made to any
other than the original consignee, the name and
address of the consignor, the point of origin, the
point of delivery, the date of delivery, the number

54 Acts of the General Assembly
and initials of each tank car, if shipped by rail, the
quantity of each shipment and delivery in gallons,
the manner of shipment and delivery, and such
other information as the Department may require
relative to the transportation and delivery of such
gasoline. Such reports are to include intra-city
switching movements of gasoline in tank cars or
otherwise. Such reports shall be made under oath
and shall be filed on or before the last day of every
month covering all such deliveries made within the
State during the preceding calendar month.
Section Eight (8). Report of sales by counties.
Every dealer shall on or before the last day of the
month file a report with the Department showing
the total number of gallons sold, distributed or used
in each county of the state during the next preced
ing calendar month.
Section Nine (9). Reports required on gasoline
received. Every dealer or the treasurer or other
proper officer or agent of every dealer shall on or
before the last day of each month transmit to the
Department a statement under oath or affirmation
on such forms as the Department may prescribe of
the total number of gallons of gasoline received as
the term ' ' received ' ' is hereinbefore defined in the
State of Kentucky during the next preceding cal
endar month and the statement shall include the fol
lowing information :
a. An itemized statement of the number of gal
lons of all gasoline received during the next pre
ceding calendar month by such dealer which has
been produced, refined, manufactured or com
pounded by such dealer in the State of Kentucky.
b. An itemized statement of the number of gal
lons of all gasoline received by such dealer in the
State of Kentucky from any source whatsoever
during the next preceding calendar month as shown

Chapter 6 55
by shippers' invoices thereof, other than the gas
oline falling within the provisions of (a) above, to
gether with a statement showing the date of receipt
of such gasoline, the name of the person from whom
purchased, the date of receipt of each shipment of
gasoline, the point of origin and the point of desti
nation, the quantity of each of said purchases or
shipment, the name of the carrier, the initials and
number of each tank car, date of receipt and the
number of gallons contained in each car if shipped
by rail or the name and owner of the boat, ship,
truck, transport, barge or vessel if shipped by
water.
c. An itemized statement of the number of gal
lons of received gasoline, if any, which such dealer
has during the preceding calendar month :
(1) Sold to the United States Government or
any of its agencies.
(2) Sold for delivery in Kentucky in transport
truck, tank car or cargo lots to licensed bonded
dealers. Statement shall give a record of all such
transport truck, tank car or cargo sales of gasoline,
giving the date of shipment, the number of gallons
contained in each shipment, the name of owner and
license number of truck, if shipped by transport
truck, the initials and number of the tank car if
shipped by rail, the name and owner of the boat,
barge or vessel and the number of gallons contained
therein, if shipped by water, and shall set forth the
name of the person to whom sold, point of ship
ment and point of delivery.
(3) Lost through accountable losses which have
been reported to and approved in writing by the
Department as provided in Section 1-g of this Act.
(4) Exported or sold for export from the State
of Kentucky to any other state or to any foreign
country.

56 Acts of the General Assembly
d. Such other information as may be required
by the Department.
Section Ten (10). Receipt and tax on inventory.
In addition to the data required in Section 9 the re
port rendered by any dealer for the first calendar
month from and after the date when this Act shall
have become effective shall contain a statement of
the number of gallons of gasoline on hand, untaxed
and received (within the meaning of the term "re
ceived" as defined in Section 1-e of this Act), by
such dealers as of the date when this Act shall have
become effective. Such gasoline shall be deemed
to have been received by such dealer in six equal
quantities in the first six calendar months following
the effective date of this Act, one-sixth of the total
quantity in each month and each one-sixth shall be
included in computing the tax payable by said dealer
for each of said six months.
Section Eleven (11). Computation and payment
of tax.
a. From the total number of gallons of gasoline
received by the dealer within the State of Kentucky
during the next preceding calendar month, the fol
lowing deductions shall be made:
(1) The total number of gallons of gasoline re
ceived by the dealer within the State of Kentucky
and sold or otherwise disposed of during the next
preceding calendar month as set forth in Section 9,
paragraph (c), items (1)-(2)-(3) and (4) of this
Act.
(2) That number of gallons of gasoline which
shall be equal to two and one-fourth (2%%) per
cent of the total number of gallons of gasoline re
ceived by the dealer within the State of Kentucky
during the next preceding calendar month, less the
total number of gallons deducted under sub-para
graph (1) next preceding; this deduction being

Chapter 6 5?
allowed to cover evaporation, shrinkage, unaccount
able losses, collection costs, bad debts, handling and
reporting the tax.
b. The tax at the rate of five cents (5c) per gal
lon of gasoline shall be computed on the number of
gallons remaining after the deductions hereinabove
set forth have been made and shall constitute the
amount of gasoline tax payable for the next pre
ceding calendar month.
Reports required in Section 8 shall be accom
panied by a certified or cashier's check, payable to
the State Treasurer of Kentucky for the amount of
tax due for such preceding calendar month.
Section Twelve (12). Failure to report and pay
tax, penalties provided. If any dealer neglects or
refuses to make said return, or pay the tax at the
time hereinbefore provided, the amount thereof,
with an addition of a penalty of twenty per cent
(20%) thereof, with interest at the rate of six per
cent (6%) per annum from the date when due until
paid, shall be paid on an account settled by the De
partment, as other taxes are paid, settled and col
lected ; and, if collected by proceedings in any court,
an additional penalty of twenty per cent (20%)
shall be collected as is authorized to be collected
under Section 4263-4 of the Kentucky Statutes, as
amended by House Bill Number One, First Ex
traordinary Session of 1936.
The said twenty per cent (20%) penalty thereon
and interest due shall be paid into the State Treas
ury as part of the tax, and the additional twenty
per cent (20%) penalty collected by suit shall be
distributed as provided in Section 4263-4 of the
Kentucky Statutes, as amended by House Bill Num
ber One, First Extraordinary Session of 1936.
Section Thirteen (13). Penalties for violation.
By virtue of the allowance provided herein to deal

58 Acts op the General Assembly
ers for collecting the tax and remitting the same to
the Department, every dealer is a trust officer of the
State, and failure to remit gasoline tax to the State
as provided herein constitutes embezzlement of
State funds, and upon conviction under this section
any individual, or partner, or officer, or agent of any
association, partnership or corporation shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor and shall be fined not ex
ceeding one thousand dollars ($1,000) or impris
oned in the county jail not exceeding six months, or
both so fined and imprisoned, and upon conviction
on a second offense under this section, shall be
guilty of a felony and shall be imprisoned for not
less than one year or more than five years in the
State prison. Provided that in case such violator
be other than natural person then the principal
officer and/or the officer or officers responsible for
such violation may be punished by such imprison
ment.
Section Fourteen (14). Powers of the Depart
ment of Revenue. The administration of this Act
is vested in the Department of Revenue, which may
prescribe forms and promulgate rules and regula
tions needed for the enforcement of this Act.
The Department, through a representative ap
pointed by it in writing, is hereby authorized to ex
amine the books, records, papers, files, equipment
and/ or inventory of any person made liable for any
tax under the provisions of this Act; to examine
witnesses under oath for purposes appropriate for
enforcement of this Act; and to examine the books,
records, papers, files, equipment and/or inventory
of manufacturers, wholesalers, retailers, or carriers
for the purpose of determining whether the Act is
being violated.
The Department shall have the power to insti
tute legal proceedings by the Attorney General of

Chapter 6 59
Kentucky, or any attorney duly authorized by him,
for the purpose of ascertaining the amount of tax
due and/or enforcing the collection thereof with
penalties and interest thereon, and for the purpose
of enjoining the operation of the business of the
delinquent.
The Department may at or after the commence
ment of an action for the purpose of collecting the
amount of tax due and penalty and interest thereon,
as set out herein, have an attachment against the
property of the person liable for tax or garnishment
of his creditors without the execution of bond on the
filing of an affidavit showing the nature of the claim,
that it is just, the sum which the Department be
lieves the Commonwealth should recover, and any
of the grounds provided by the Civil Code of Prac
tice for the issuance of an attachment in civil causes.
Section Fifteen (15). Information privileged.
Any information obtained by the Department or
any agent or representative thereof as a result of a
report, investigation, or verification herein author
ized to be made, shall be confidential and any person
divulging any such information except upon order
of a court of competent jurisdiction or to an officer
of the Commonwealth entitled to receive the same
in his official capacity shall be fined not less than
twenty-five dollars ($25.00) nor more than five hun
dred dollars ($500.00).
Section Sixteen (16). Restraining suits. No suit
shall be maintained in any court to restrain or de
lay the collection of the tax herein imposed on any
grounds whatever but the aggrieved taxpayer shall
pay under protest the tax as and when due and may
at any time within two years after such payment
sue the Commonwealth through its agent, the
Auditor of Public Accounts, in any action in any
court, State or Federal, otherwise having jurisdic-
Acts— 3

60 Acts of the General Assembly
tion of the subject matter for the recovery of the
tax so paid with legal interest thereon from the
date of payment. If it can be finally determined
that said tax or any part thereof was wrongfully
collected for any reason it shall be the duty of the
Auditor of Public Accounts to issue his warrant on
the Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
for the amount of such tax so adjudged to have been
wrongfully collected, together with interest there
on; and the Treasurer shall pay the same at once
out of the Road Fund of the State in preference to
other warrants or claims against the Common
wealth.
Separate suits need not be filed for each separate
payment by any taxpayer but recovery may be had
in one for all suits heretofore paid or not barred by
the limitation of two years herein imposed.
Section Seventeen (17). Penalties for failure to
make returns, to pay tax, or to permit examination
of records. Any person or dealer who shall fail,
neglect or refuse to make the returns and pay the
tax herein prescribed, or shall refuse to permit the
Department, its assistants, agents or representa
tives appointed by it in writing, to examine the
books, records, papers, files and equipment of such
person or dealer pertaining to the business made
taxable by this Act, or who makes any incomplete,
false or fraudulent return hereunder, or who does,
or attempts to do, anything whatever to avoid a
full disclosure of the amount of business done or to
avoid the payment of the whole or any part of the
tax or penalties due, or who shall fail to keep and
preserve records of gasoline manufactured, trans
ported, received, used, sold or delivered or make re
ports as provided in this Act, shall be guilty of a
misdemeanor, and upon conviction, shall be fined not
less than twenty-five dollars ($25.00) nor more than

Chapter 6 61
one thousand dollars ($1,000), and in the case the
offender is an individual, he may be imprisoned in
the county jail not exceeding six months, or both so
fined and imprisoned. Provided, that in case such
violator be other than a natural person then the
principal officer and/or the officer or officers re
sponsible for such violation, may be punished by
such imprisonment. Such penalty shall be in addi
tion to the penalty imposed hereinbefore in Section
13 of this Act.
Section Eighteen (18). Dealers license required.
After this Act shall go into effect, it shall be unlaw
ful for any dealer to receive, use, sell or distribute
any gasoline or to engage in the sale of gasoline
within this State unless such dealer is the holder
of an uncancelled license issued by the Department
to engage in such business. To procure such license
every dealer shall file with the Department an ap
plication under oath or affirmation and in such form
as the Department may prescribe, setting forth:
a. The name under which the dealer will transact
business within the State of Kentucky;
b. The location, with street number, address, of
its principal office or place of business within this
State; and if such dealer has more than one place
of business within the State, the locations of each
place of business;
c. The name and complete residence address of
the owner or the names and addresses of the part
ners, if such dealer is a partnership, or the names
and addresses of the principal officers, if such
dealer is a corporation or association; and if such
dealer is a corporation organized under the laws of
another- state, territory or country, it shall also file
with such application a certified copy of the certifi
cate or license issued by the Secretary of State of
Kentucky showing that such corporation is author

62 Acts of the General Assembly
ized to transact business in the State of Kentucky.
Upon the filing of an application for a license, and
concurrently therewith, a bond of the character
stipulated and in the amount provided for in Sec
tion 19 of this Act shall be filed with the Depart
ment. No license shall be issued upon any applica
tion unless accompanied by such a bond. In the
event that any application for a license certificate to
transact business as a dealer in the State of Ken
tucky shall be filed by any person whose license
shall at any time heretofore have been cancelled for
cause by the Department, or in case said Depart
ment shall be of the opinion that such application
is not filed in good faith, or that such application
is filed by some person as a subterfuge for the real
person in interest whose license or registration shall
theretofore have been cancelled for cause by said
Department, then and in any of said events, the
Department, after a hearing, of which the applicant,
shall have been given five (5) days' notice in writ
ing and in which said applicant shall have the right
to appear in person or by counsel and present testi
mony, shall have and is hereby given the right and
authority to refuse to issue to such person a license
certificate to transact business as a dealer in the
State of Kentucky. The application in proper form
having been accepted for filing, and the bond having
been accepted and approved, the Department shall
issue to such dealer a license in the State of Ken
tucky, subject to cancellation of such license as pro
vided by law. The license certificate so issued by
the Department shall not be assignable, and shall
be valid only for the dealer in whose name issued,
and shall be displayed conspicuously in the prin
cipal place of business of said dealer in the State
of Kentucky. The Department shall keep and file
all applications and bonds with an alphabetical index

Chapter 6 63
thereof, together with a record of all licensed
dealers. It shall be the duty of the Department to
publish and keep currently up to date a list of
licensed dealers and transmit a copy of such list and
all revisions thereto to all licensed dealers.
Section Nineteen (19). Bond required. Every
dealer shall file with the Department a bond in the
minimum amount of $2,000.00, or an amount equal
to three times the monthly tax liability, whichever
shall be less, and up to a maximum amount suf
ficient in the opinion of the Department, the financial
rating and reputation of the company considered,
to insure payment to the State of Kentucky of the
amount of tax and penalties and interest for which
the dealer may become liable, on a form to be ap
proved by the Department and with a surety thereon
approved by the Department and upon which such
dealer shall be the principal obligor and the State
of Kentucky shall be the obligee, conditioned upon
the prompt filing of true reports and the payment
by such dealer to the Treasurer of the State of
Kentucky of any and all gasoline excise taxes which
are now or which hereafter may be levied or im
posed by the State of Kentucky together with any
and all penalties and/or interest thereon, and gen
erally upon faithful compliance with the provisions
of this Act. In the event that liability upon the bond
thus filed by the dealer with the Department shall
be discharged or reduced, whether by judgment
rendered, payment made or otherwise, or if in the
opinion of the Department any surety on the bond
theretofore given shall have become unsatisfactory
or unacceptable, then the Department may require
the dealer to file a new bond with satisfactory
sureties in the same amount, failing which the De
partment shall forthwith cancel the license certifi
cate of said dealer. If such new bond shall be

64 Acts of the General Assembly
furnished by said dealer as above provided, the De
partment shall cancel and surrender the bond of
said dealer for which such new bond shall be sub
stituted. In the event that upon hearing, of which
the dealer shall be given five (5) days' notice in
writing, the Department shall decide that the amount
of the existing bond is insufficient to insure payment
to the State of Kentucky of the amount of the tax
and any penalties and interest for which said dealer
is or may at any time become liable, then the dealer
shall forthwith, upon the written demand of the
Department file an additional bond in the same
manner and form with a surety thereon approved
by the Department to be necessary to secure at all
times the payment by such dealer to the State of
Kentucky of all taxes, penalties, and interest due
under1 the provisions of this Act, failing which the
Department shall forthwith cancel the license cer
tificate of said dealers. Any surety on any bond
furnished by any dealer as above provided shall be
released and discharged from any and all liability
to the State of Kentucky accruing on such bond
after the expiration of sixty (60) days from the
date upon which such surety shall have lodged with
the Department written request to be released and
discharged. Provided, however, that such request
shall not operate to relieve, release or discharge
such surety from any liability already accrued, or
which shall accrue, before the expiration of said
sixty day period. The Department shall promptly
upon receipt of notice of such request notify the
dealer who furnished such bond, and, unless, such
dealer shall on or before the expiration of such
sixty day period file with the Department a new
bond with a surety satisfactory to the Department
in the amount and form hereinbefore in this section
provided, the Department shall forthwith cancel the

Chapter 6 65
license of said dealer. If such new bond shall be
furnished by said dealer as above provided, the De
partment shall cancel and surrender the bond of
said dealer for which such new bond shall be sub
stituted.
Section Twenty (20). Cancellation of license. If
any dealer shall at any time file a false monthly re
port of the data or information required by this Act,
or shall fail, refuse or neglect to file the monthly
report required by this Act, or to pay the full
amount of the tax as required by this Act, the De
partment may forthwith cancel the license of said
dealer and notify such dealer in writing of such
cancellation by registered mail to the last known
address of such dealer appearing on the files of the
Department. The Department is hereby given the
power to cancel any license hitherto or hereafter
issued to any dealer, such cancellation to become
effective sixty (60) days from the date of receipt of
the written request of such dealer for cancellation
thereof or said Department may cancel the license
of any dealer upon investigation and sixty (60)
days' notice mailed to the last known address of
such dealer if it shall ascertain and find that the
person to whom such license has been issued is no
longer engaged in the receipt, use or sale of gasoline
as a dealer, and has not been so engaged for the
period of six (6) months prior to such cancellation.
But no such license shall be cancelled upon the re
quest of any dealer until and unless the dealer shall,
prior to the date of such cancellation, have paid to
the State of Kentucky all excise taxes payable
under the laws of the State of Kentucky together
with any and all penalties and fines accruing by
reason of any failure on the part of said dealer to
make accurate reports as required by this Act
and/or pay such taxes and/or penalties. In the

66 Acts of the General Assembly
event that the license of any dealer shall be can
celled by the Department as hereinbefore in this
section provided, and in the further' event that said
dealer shall have paid to the State of Kentucky
upon the receipt, sale or use of gasoline, together
with any and all penalties accruing by reason of
any failure on the part of said dealer to make ac
curate reports or to pay said tax and/or penalties,
then the Department shall cancel and surrender the
bond heretofore filed by said dealer.
Section Twenty-One (21). Penalty for operation
without license. Any person who shall engage in
the business of receiving, using, selling, and/or dis
tributing gasoline in the State of Kentucky as a
dealer without being the holder of an uncancelled
license to engage in such business, or who shall,
without such license, receive, use, sell and/or dis
tribute any gasoline upon which the tax herein im
posed shall not have been reported and paid, shall
pay a penalty equal to twenty per cent (20%) of
the amount of the tax, and, in addition thereto, shall
be guilty of a misdemeanor and upon conviction
thereof shall be punished by a fine of not less than
one hundred dollars ($100.00) nor more than one
thousand dollars ($1,000.00) or by imprisonment in
the county jail for not less than thirty (30) days
nor more than one (1) year or both so fined and im
prisoned in the discretion of the jury. Each day
or part thereof during which any person shall en
gage in business as a dealer without being the hold
er of an uncancelled license shall constitute a sep
arate offense within the meaning of this section.
Provided that in case such violator be other than a
natural person, then the principal officer and/or the
officer or officers responsible for such violation may
be punished by such imprisonment.
Section Twenty-two (22). Exchange of informa

Chapter 7 67
Hon with other states. The Department shall, upon
request duly received from the officials to whom are
intrusted the enforcement of the gasoline tax laws
of any other state, forward to such officials any in
formation which it may have in its possession rela
tive to the manufacture, receipt, sale, use, transpor
tation and/or shipment by any person of gasoline.
Provided, however, this section shall not apply un
less such state extend to this State a similar priv
ilege.
Section Twenty-three (23). Separability clause.
The provisions of this Act are severable, and if any
of its provisions or any penalty provided by this
Act shall be held unconstitutional, such provision
or the particular penalty held to be invalid shall not
affect or impair the remainder of the Act or such
part of the penalties as are not held to be invalid
and the decisions of the courts shall not affect or
impair the remaining provisions of this Act or any
of the other penalties anywhere in this Act pro
vided. It is hereby declared as a legislative intent
that this Act would have been adopted had any un
constitutional provisions of such invalid penalties
not been included herein.
Section Twenty-four (24). Emergency. Where
as, the present State revenue does not meet the im
mediate needs for maintenance of the State govern
ment an emergency is declared to exist and this Act
shall become a law and be effective upon its pas
sage and approval by the Governor.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation, providing for an
income tax, the collection and administration thereof, and
declaring an emegency.
Approved by the Governor.
CHAPTER 7.

68 Acts of the General, Assembly
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky. .
Section One (1). Definitions. For the purpose
of this Act, and unless otherwise required by the
context :
(a) The word "person" includes individuals, co
partnerships, business trusts, joint stock companies,
fiduciaries, and corporations.
(b) The word "taxpayer" includes any person,
corporation, partnership, association, trust or estate
required by this Act to pay tax and/or to file re
ports.
(c) The word "partnership" includes all co
partnerships, whether general or special, and wheth
er limited or unlimited; it includes also all joint en
terprises.
(d) The word "individual" means all natural
persons whether married or unmarried, and also all
trusts, estates and fiduciaries acting for other per
sons; it does not include corporations or partner
ships acting for or in their own behalf.
(e) The words "taxable year" mean the calen
dar year or the fiscal year ending during such calen
dar year upon the basis of which the net income is
computed under this Act. The term ' ' taxable year
' '
includes, in the case of a return made for a frac
tional part of a year under the provisions of this
Act, the period for which such return is made.
(f) The words "fiscal year" mean an account
ing period of twelve (12) months ending on any day
of any month other than December 31.
(g) The word "resident" applies only to nat
ural persons and includes, for the purpose of deter
mining liability to the taxes imposed by this Act
upon the income of any taxable year, every person
domiciled in this State on the last day of the tax
able year, and every other person who, for more

Chapter 7 69
than six (6) months of the taxable year, maintained
his place of abode within this State, whether domi
ciled in this State or not. But any person who,
on or before the last day of the taxable year, changes
his place of abode to a place without this State, with
the bona fide intention of continuing actually to
abide permanently without this State, shall be tax
able the same as a non-resident is taxable under
this Act. The fact that a person who has changed
his place of abode, within six (6) months from so
doing, again resides within this State, shall be prima
facie evidence that he did not intend permanently to
have his place of abode without this State. Such
person not having returned his income for taxation
as a resident of this State shall be deemed to have
resided on the day when such income should have
been listed at his last place of abode within this
State. The fact that a person whose place of abode
during the greater portion of such twelve (12)
months has been within this State does not claim
or exercise the right to vote at public elections
within this State, shall not, of itself, constitute him
a non-resident of this State within the meaning of
this Act.
(h) The word "dividend" means any distribu
tion made by a corporation out of its earnings or
profits to its shareholders or members whether such
distribution be made in cash, or in other property,
other than stock in the corporation. It includes
such portion of the assets of a corporation distrib
uted at the time of dissolution as are in effect a
distribution of earnings.
(i) The word "include" when used in a defini
tion contained in this Act shall not be deemed to ex
clude other things otherwise within the meaning of
the term defined.
(j) The word "Department" means the De

70 Acts of the General Assembly
partment of Kevenue of Kentucky, and "Commis
sioner" means the Commissioner of Revenue.
Section Two (2). Gross income and exclusions
therefrom. The term "gross income," as used
herein, includes gains, profits and income derived
from salaries, wages, or compensation for personal
services of whatever kind and in whatever form
paid, or from professions, vocations, trades, busi
nesses, commerce, or sales or dealings in property,
whether real or personal, growing out of the owner
ship, or use, or interest in such property (but in
come realized or loss sustained from the sale of
property held two years or more, other than prop
erty items customarily included in an inventory,
shall be ignored in computing income for purposes
of this Act) ; also from rent, royalties, interest,
dividends, securities or transactions of any business
carried on for gain or profit, or gains or profits and
income derived from any source whatever, including
gains or profits and income derived through estates
or trusts by the beneficiaries thereof, whether as
distributive or as distributable shares. The amount
of all such items shall be included in the gross in
come for the taxable year in which received by the
taxpayer, unless under the methods of accounting
permitted herein, such amounts are to be properly
accounted for as of a different period; but the term
"gross income" does not include the following
items, which shall be exempt from taxation under
(a) The proceeds of life insurance policies paid
by reason of the death of the insured, but interest
thereon shall be assessed under the provisions of
this Act;
(b) Amounts received (other than amounts paid
by reason of the death of the insured) under life in
surance, endowment, or annuity contracts, either

Chapter 7 71
during the term or at maturity or upon surrender
of the contract, equal to the total amount of pre
miums paid thereon ; provided, however, that in the
case of annuities either for life or for a fixed term,
the excess value thereof over amount of premiums
paid, derived through application of the life expec
tancy (under the United States Mortality Tables)
or the number of years of the fixed term, shall not
be exempt and shall, for the purpose of computing
the annual taxable income, be divided by the number
of years such annunties are payable. The annual
income so computed shall be taxable as is other in
come.
(c) The value of property acquired by gift,
quest, .devise or inheritance received during the
year; but the income received by reason of such
gifts, bequests, devises and inheritances shall be
assessed under the provisions of this Act ;
(d) Any amount received through accident or
health insurance or under the workmen's compen
sation act for personal injuries or sickness, and the
amount of any damages, received, whether by suit
or agreement, on account of such injuries or sick
ness;
(e) Interest upon the obligations of the United
States or any instrumentalities of the Federal Gov
ernment, and interest upon the obligations of this
State or any of its political subdivisions and munic
ipalities and instrumentalities thereof ;
Section Three (3). Deductions. (1) Taxpay
ers reporting income as prescribed by this Act shall
be allowed the following deductions ;
(a) All ordinary and necessary expenses paid
or incurred during the taxable year in carrying on
any trade or business, including a reasonable allow
ance for salaries or other compensation paid for

72 Acts of the General Assembly
personal services actually rendered in producing
the taxable income;
(b) All interest paid or accrued within the tax
able year, except on indebtedness incurred to pur
chase or continued to carry property, the income
from which is exempt from taxation under this Act.
(c) Property taxes, poll taxes, and franchise or
license taxes paid to the Commonwealth of Ken
tucky, its taxing subdivisions, or its municipalities ;
and income taxes paid to the United States.
(d) Losses sustained during the taxable year and
not compensated for by insurance or otherwise, if
incurred in trade or business, the income from which
is subject to taxation under this Act;
(e) Losses sustained during the taxable year and
not compensated for by insurance or otherwise, if
incurred in any transaction entered into for profit
which profit would be subject to taxation under this
Act, though not connected with the trade or busi
ness;
(f) Losses sustained during the taxable year of
property not connected with the trade or business
(but in the case of a taxpayer other than a resident,
only of real property or tangible personal property
having a permanent situs in this State), if arising
from fire, storms, shipwrecks or other casualty or
from theft and not compensated for by insurance
or otherwise;
(g) Debts ascertained to be worthless and charged
off within the taxable year. In the case of a debt
existing on the effective date of this Act no more
than its fair market value on that date shall be de
ducted. A worthless debt arising after the effective
date of this Act from unpaid wages, salary, sales,
rent or any similar item of taxable income is not an
allowable deduction unless the income which said
item represents has been included as income by the

Chapter 7 73
taxpayer in a return rendered under this Act. In
the case of taxpayers who keep regular books of
account on an accrual basis and according to stand
ard accounting practice, there may be deducted, in
lieu of the foregoing, additions to reserves for bad
debts, under such rules as the Department may pre
scribe; if said Department at any time shall deem
the reserve excessive, it may restore such excess to
income either in a subsequent year or as a part of
the income of the taxable year and assess it
accordingly ;
(h) The actual amount paid during the taxable
year for repairs to and maintenance of buildings
and machinery: provided, that no deductions shall
be made for any repair which is chargeable to resi
dence property occupied by its owner, or for de
preciation thereof ; and the annual value of the esti
mated rental thereof shall not be included in the
income subject to taxation.
(i) All fire, tornado and casualty insurance
premiums paid during the taxable year in connec
tion with property held for investment or business.
(j) A reasonable allowance for exhaustion, wear
and tear of property used in the trade or business,
including a reasonable allowance for obsolescence
and including in the case of natural resources allow
ances for depletion equivalent to those allowed
under the Federal Income Tax law in effect at the
date of the passage of this Act.
(k) Dividends received during the taxable year
upon stock of national banks and dividends received
during the taxable year upon stock of banks and
trust companies organized under the laws of this
State.
(1) Contributions or gifts made by individuals
within the taxable year to or for the use of : (1) this
State, its institutions, or any municipality or taxing

74 Acts of the General Assembly
subdivision of the State for exclusively public pur
poses; (2) any corporation, or trust, or community
chest, fund, or foundation, organized and operated
exclusively for religious, charitable, scientific, lit
