Section Seven (7). (1) The Department of Reve
nue shall prepare and furnish, upon applications,
blank forms covering such information as may be
necessary to determine the amount of inheritance
tax due the State on the transfer of all property
subject to tax; it shall determine the value of such
property and have full authority to do all things
necessary to secure full and final settlement of all
such inheritance taxes due, or to become due, to
the State Treasury.
(2) All taxes imposed by this Act, unless other
wise herein provided for, shall be due and payable
at the death of the decedent, and if the same are
paid within nine months a discount of five per
centum (5%) shall be allowed, and if the same are
paid within eighteen months no interest shall be
charged and collected thereon, but if not so paid
within eighteen months, a penalty at the rate of ten
per centum (10%) per annum shall be charged and
collected from the time said taxes accrued. And in
all cases where the executors, administrators, or
trustees do not pay such taxes within eighteen
months from the death of the decedent, they shall be
required to give bond in the form and to the effect
prescribed by the Department of Revenue for the
payment of said taxes together with interest.
(3) The penalty of ten per centum (10%) per
annum imposed for the non-payment of said tax
shall not be charged in cases where, by reason of
claims made upon the estate, necessary litigation,
or other unavoidable cause of delay, the estate of

120 Acts of the General Assembly
any decedent, or a part thereof, cannot be settled at
the end of eighteen months from the death of the de
cedent; and in such case only six per centum (6%)
per annum shall be charged upon the said tax from
the expiration of said eighteen months until the
cause of such delay is removed.
(4) The personal representative of any estate
from which any such tax is or may be due may make
an estimate agreeable to the Department of Revenue
and pay the same to the said Department, which
shall receipt therefor, at any time before the same
is determined, and shall thereupon be entitled to any
discount and be relieved of any interest and penalty
and shall have permits issued for the transfer of
securities or real estate upon the amount so paid in
the same manner as if the tax were then determined.
Any excess so paid shall be refunded to the person
so paying or entitled thereto by such Auditor out of
the general expenditure fund, upon filing with such
Auditor a copy of the order fixing such tax, and at
tached thereto a certificate stating the amount of
refund due.
(5) In the absence of administration in this State
upon the estate of a non-resident, the Department
of Revenue, at the request of an executor or ad
ministrator duly appointed and qualified in the state
of the decedent's domicile, or of a grantee under a
conveyance made during the grantor's lifetime, and
upon satisfactory evidence furnished him by such
executor, administrator, or grantee, or otherwise,
may determine whether or not any property of said
decedent within this State is subject to the provi
sions of this Act, and if so, may determine the value
of such tax and adjust the same with such executor,
administrator, or grantee, and for that purpose may
appoint an appraiser to appraise said property and
the expense of such appraisal shall be charged upon

Chapter 8 121
Such property in addition to the tax. The Depart
ment's certificate as to the amount of such tax and
its receipt for the amount therein certified may be
filed in the county court in the county where the

dence of the payment of the tax, to the extent of such
certification. Whenever in such a case the tax is
not adjusted within six months after the death of the

ministrator in this State.
Section Eight (8). (1) No safe deposit company,

ceptacle without first requiring all persons given

ceptacle, upon the death of any other person having

ed, of the death of such person ; and it shall be un

tody or partial custody of any safe deposit box or

nue or a person by it authorized to issue such con

curities, deposits, assets or property belonging to

(3) An executor, administrator, or trustee, hold

upon land or the proceeds of insurance policies which

cedent in his lifetime is subject to said tax, and the

without the State, or is removed from the State be
fore the tax is paid, such tax shall become a lien
upon all property of the decedent ; and the executor
or administrator shall collect taxes due on account
of such conveyance and may be authorized to sell
any property subject to the lien of such tax, for the
payment thereof, as in the other cases.
(4) If a legacy subject to said tax is charged upon
or payable out of real estate, the heir or devisee,
before paying
it,shall deduct said tax therefrom
and pay
itto the executor, adminstrator or trustee.
The payment of this tax shall be enforced in the

Chapter 8 125
same manner as the payment of a tax on a direct
legacy itself could be enforced.
(5) When any interest in property less than an
estate in fee is devised or bequeathed to one or more
beneficiaries with remainder to others and the in
terest of one or more beneficiaries is subject to said
tax, the executor shall deduct the tax upon such
taxable interests from the whole property thus de
vised or bequeathed and whenever property other
than money is so devised or bequeathed he may,
unless the taxes upon all the taxable interests are
paid when due by the beneficiaries, be authorized to
sell such property or such portion thereof as may
be necessary, as provided in sub-section (3) of this
section, and having deducted the unpaid taxes on

sale, he shall account for the balance in lieu of the
property sold as in other cases.
(6) The official or body charged with the admin
istration of the death tax laws of the domiciliary
state shall be deemed a creditor of the decedent and
shall be entitled to sue in the courts of this State
and to enforce such claim for taxes, penalties and/or
interest due to such state or political subdivision.
The provisions of this subdivision shall apply to
the estate of the decedent not domiciled in this State
only if the laws of his domicile contain a provision,
of any nature, however expressed, whereby this
State is given reasonable assurance of the collection
of its death taxes, interest and penalties from the
estates of decedents dying domiciled in this State.
Section Nine (9). In case of a false or fraudulent
return, or of the omission to fairly return, or in
case of the failure to include in a return any prop
erty of a decedent subject to death tax under this
Act, or under any prior Act, whether or not omis
sion is with intent to evade the tax, the death tax

126 Acts of the General Assembly
due by reason thereof may a any time notwithstand
ing any provision of limitation elsewhere made and
provided be assessed and collected by the Depart
ment of Revenue by the methods set out in this Act,
including action in a court of competent jurisdic
tion.
Section Ten (10). Every person who willfully and
knowingly subscribes to or makes any false state
ment of facts, or knowingly subscribes to or ex
hibits any false paper or false report with intent
to deceive any appraiser pursuant to the provisions
of this Act, shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and
punished by a fine of not less than Fifty Dollars
($50.00) nor more than Five Hundred Dollars
($500.00) or imprisoned not exceeding twelve months
in the county jail, or by both such fine and jail sen
tence.
Any person charged with any duty under the
terms of this Act who fails or refuses to perform
the same shall be guilty of a misdemeanor, and shall
be fined not less than Five Dollars ($5.00) nor more
than One Hundred Dollars ($100.00).
Section Eleven (11). The provisions of this Act
are severable. If any provision, section, or para
graph thereof, or if the application of any provision,
section, or paragraph to any transfer affected by the
operation of the Act, should be held unconstitutional
or invalid, such decision regarding the provision,
section, paragraph, or application, shall not affect
or impair the remainder of the Act, it being hereby
declared the legislative intent to tax separately the
transfer of each class of propery contemplated by
any of its provisions.
Section Twelve (12). All tax collected under this
Act shall be covered through the Department of
Finance into the State treasury and credited to the
general expenditure fund.

Section Thirteen (13). All laws or parts of laws
in conflict herewith are hereby repealed to the extent
of such conflict.
Section Fourteen (14). Emergency. Whereas,
the present state revenue does not meet the immedi
ate needs for the maintenance of the State govern
ment, an emergency is declared to exist and this Act
shall become a law and be effective upon its passage
and approval by the Governor.

AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation imposing a license
tax on race tracks at which horse racing shall be conducted
for any stake, purse or prize under the jurisdiction of the
State Racing Commission, which license tax shall be in lieu
Approved April 24, 1936.
CHAPTER 9.
Approved —
CHAPTER 10.

128 Acts or the General Assembly
of certain other taxes to the Commonwealth o£ Kentucky or
any county, city, town, municipality or other political sub
division thereof; also imposing a tax of fifteen cents on ad
missions to said race tracks, with certain exceptions, and
providing for the collection of said tax by said race track and
providing for reports to the Department of Revenue in con
nection therewith and providing for the payment thereof and
repealing all laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith, and
declaring an emergency to exist.
WHEREAS the Legislature finds as a fact that a race track
located in a county of two hundred thousand (200,000) popu
lation is able financially by reason of such population to pay
a higher license tax than race tracks located in counties of
less population, and
WHEREAS the Legislature finds as a fact that a race track
located in a county of seventy-two thousand (72,000) and
under two hundred thousand (200,000) population is able
financially by reason of such population to pay a higher
license tax than race tracks located in counties of less popu
lation, and
WHEREAS the Legislature finds as a fact that race tracks in
all other counties are unable financially to pay the same
license tax as race tracks located in counties of greater popu
lation than seventy-two thousand (72,000) by reason of said
population.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Section One (1). That every person, firm, fidu
ciary, association or corporation engaged in the busi
ness of conducting a race track at which races are
run for stakes, purses or prizes, under the jurisdic
tion of the Kentucky State Racing Commission, shall
pay to the Commonwealth of Kentucky a license tax
therefor, as follows :
Section Two (2). Any such race track located
within a county with a population of two hundred
thousand (200,000) or over, shall pay for each day
upon which races are conducted at such track for any
stake, purse or prize, a license tax of twenty-five
hundred dollars ($2,500.00) per day, and any such

Chapter 10
track located within a county having a population
of not less than seventy-two thousand (72,000) and
not more than two hundred thousand (200,000),
shall pay for each day upon which races are con
ducted at said track for any stake, purse or prize,
a license tax of one thousand dollars ($1,000.00) per
day, and in all other counties any such race tracks
located therein shall pay for each day upon which
races are conducted for any stake, purse or prize,
a license tax of five hundred dollars ($500.00) per
day.
Section Three (3). Each person entering the
grounds or enclosure of any race track whereat a
race meeting is being conducted, for the purpose of
attending the races, or for any other purpose con
nected therewith, under the jurisdiction of the Ken
tucky State Racing Commission, shall pay a tax of
fifteen cents (15c) to the Commonwealth of Ken
tucky, except as herein provided for. If tickets good
for more than one day are issued then the sum of
fifteen cents (15c) shall be paid by each person
using such ticket on each day that the same shall be
used; provided, however, that no admission tax shall
be collected from or charged any of the employees
of the said race track, or any of the owners or train
ers of horses, or jockeys, or their employees. The
admission tax herein provided for shall be collected
by the said race track from each person on entering
the race track or enclosure on a paid or free admis
sion, and said race track shall account to and pay
to the Commonwealth of Kentucky the money so
collected from persons subject to the tax herein pro
vided.
Section Four (4). It shall be the duty of each per
son, firm, fiduciary, association or corporation en
gaged in the business of conducting a race track to
furnish the Commissioner of Revenue, within thirty

130 Acts of the General Assembly
(30) days after the end of each race meeting, a veri
fied report of the number of days on which races
were conducted on the said race track during the
meeting, and at the same time a verified report of
the number of persons (subject to the tax levied
herein), entering the grounds or enclosure during
the race meeting, and at the same time pay to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky the correct amount due
to the Commonwealth of Kentucky by said race
track at the rate per day fixed herein and provided,
and the correct amount due to the Commonwealth
of Kentucky by said race track by reason of the
collection of the tax from persons entering the
grounds or enclosure of said race track as herein
provided for.
Section Five (5). The administration of this Act
is vested in the Department of Eevenue, which may
prescribe forms and promulgate rules and regula
tions needed for the enforcement of this Act.
The Department, through a representative ap
pointed by it in writing is hereby authorized to ex
amine the books, records, papers, files and equip
ment of any person made liable for any tax under
the provisions of this Act and to examine witnesses
under oath for purposes appropriate for enforce
ment of this Act.
The Department shall have the power to institute
legal proceedings by the Attorney General of Ken
tucky or any attorney duly authorized by him for
the purpose of ascertaining the amount of tax due
and/or enforcing the collection thereof with penal
ties and interest thereon, and for the purpose of en
joining the operation of the business of the de
linquent.
The Department may at or after the commence
ment of an action for the purpose of collecting the
amount of tax due and penalty and interest thereon,

Chapter 10 131
as set out herein, have an attachment against the
property of the person liable for tax or garnishment
of his creditors without the execution of bond on
the filing of an affidavit showing the nature of the
claim, that it is just, the sum which the Department
believes the Commonwealth should recover, and any
of the grounds provided by the Civil Code of Prac
tice for the issuance of an attachment in civil causes.
Section Six (6). Any person, firm, fiduciary, asso
ciation or corporation who shall fail to pay the tax
due in the time specified in this Act shall pay a pen
alty equal to twenty per cent ('20%) of the amount
of tax due and delinquent, together with interest at
six per cent (6%) per annum on both the original
tax and the penalty.
Any person, firm, fiduciary, association or corpo
ration who shall violate the provisions of this Act
shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and shall
be fined not more than one thousand dollars
($1,000.00) or imprisoned in the county jail not
more than thirty (30) days, or both so fined and
imprisoned. Provided, that if the offender be a
corporation the principal officer and/or the officer
or employee directly responsible for such violation
shall be punished as heretofore set out.
Section Seven (7). The license tax and admission
tax herein imposed shall be in lieu of all other
license, excise, special or franchise taxes to the Com
monwealth of Kentucky, or any county, city, town,
municipality, or other political subdivision thereof;
and no county, city, town, municipality, or other
political subdivision of the Commonwealth of Ken
tucky shall be authorized or empowered to levy or
impose any license, income, excise, special or fran
chise tax on any such person or persons, firm or
firms, association or associations or corporations en
gaged in the buiness of conducting a race track at

132 Acts of the General Assembly
which races are conducted for stakes, purses, or
prizes, or on the operation or maintenance of any
pari-mutuel machine or similar devices, or on the
sale of any commodity or merchandise during the
conducting of races thereon by any such person or
persons, firm, fiduciary, association or corporation.
Section Eight (8). All laws or parts of laws in
conflict herewith are hereby repealed to the extent
of each conflict.
Section Nine (9). Whereas the present State rev
enue does not meet the immediate needs of the main
tenance of the State government, its agencies and
subdivisions, an emergency is declared to exist and
this Act shall become a law and be effective upon its
passage and approval by the Governor.
Approved April 24, 1936.
'r. CHAPTER 11.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation, repealing, amending
and re-enacting Section 5 and Section 12 of Chapter twenty-
six (26) of the Acts of the Special Session of 1934 of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky now
codified in Baldwin's 1935 Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes, as Section 4202a-17 and Section 4202a-24, and de
claring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
Section One (1). Section 5 of the above Act is
hereby repealed, amended and re-enacted so that
when amended, and re-enacted, it shall read as fol
lows :
"Section Five (5). Every merchant establishing,
operating or maintaining one or more stores, stands
or places of business within this State, shall pay an
nually the license fee hereinafter prescribed for the
privilege of opening, establishing, operating or
maintaining such stores, stands or places of busi

Chapter 11
ness. The license fees herein prescribed shall be
as follows :
(1) On one store the flat sum of two dollars
($2.00) ;
(2) On all chains of more than one, but not more

dollars ($25:00) for each said store in excess of one;
(3) On all chains of more than five, but not more

(4) On all chains of more than ten, but hot more

lars ($352.00) plus one hundred dollars ($100.00)

(5) On all chains of more than twenty, but not

(6) On all chains of more than fifty, seven thou
sand three hundred and fifty-two dollars ($7,352.00)

pational or corporation license tax under the laws

however, that the provisions hereof shall first be ap
