Chapter 13 135
CHAPTER 13.
AN ACT relating to the licensing of motor vehicle operators
and providing for the definition of terms; and that operators
shall be licensed; persons exempt from licenses; certain ex
emptions for non-residents from license; what persons shall
not be licensed; stipulating age limits for drivers of school
buses and public passenger motor vehicles; instruction per
mits and temporary licenses; application for operator's
license or instruction permits; application of minors; designa
tion of circuit court clerks as agent of department; register
of operators to be maintained; licenses issued to operators;
duplicate license certificates; license to be signed and carried;
expiration of license; court to report convictions and suspen
sion of license; mandatory suspension or revocation of licenses
issued by the department; court or department may provide
for a hearing and appeal; right of appeal; new license after
revocations; when parent, guardian, or employer liable for
negligence of minor; owner liable for negligence of minor;
violation of license provisions; making false affidavit perjury;
unlawful for volations of Act to be permitted; or to drive
while license suspended or revoked; unlawful to drive while
license suspended or revoked; providing penalty for misde
meanor; providing penalty for driving while license suspended
or revoked; prima facie evidence of negligence in accident;
short title; constitutionality; repeal; and emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Section One (1). Definition of terms:
The following words and phrases when used in
this Act shall for the purpose of this Act have the
meanings respectively ascribed to them in this sec
tion, except in those instances where the context
clearly indicates a different meaning.
(a) "Vehicle." Every device in, upon or by
which any person or property is or may be trans
ported or drawn upon a public highway, excepting
devices moved by human and animal power or used
exclusively upon stationary rails or trucks.
(b) "Motor Vehicle." Every vehicle, as herein
denned, which is self-propelled.

136 Acts of the General Assembly
(c) "Farm Tractor." Every motor vehicle de
signed and used primarily as a farm implement for
drawing plows, sowing machines and other imple
ments of husbandry.
(d) "Person." Every natural person, firm, co
partnership, association or corporation.
(e) "Owner." A person who holds the legal
title of a vehicle or in the event a vehicle is the sub
ject of an agreement for the conditional sale or
lease thereof with the right of purchase upon per
formance of the conditions stated in the agreement
and with an immediate right of possession vested in
the conditional vendee or lessee, or in the event of a
mortgagor of a vehicle is entitled to possession, then
such conditional vendee or lessee or mortgagor shall
be deemed the owner for the purpose of this Act.
(f) "Operator." Every person, other than a
chauffeur, who is in actual physical control of a
motor vehicle upon a highway.
(g) "Non-Resident." Every person who is not
a resident of this State.
(h) "Highway." Every way or place of what
ever nature open as a matter of right to the use of
the public for purposes of vehicular travel. The
term "highway" shall not be deemed to include a
roadway or driveway upon grounds owned by pri
vate persons, colleges, universities, or other insti
tutions.
(i) "Department" shall mean the Department
of Revenue of the Commonwealth of Kentucky.
(j) "Commissioner" shall mean Commissioner
of Revenue.
Section Two (2). Operators shall be licensed:
Every person except those expressly exempted
under Sections 3, 4, and 7 of this Act before operat
ing any motor vehicle upon any highway in this
Commonwealth, shall procure an operator's license.

Chapter 13 137
The first license under this Act shall be obtained not
later than August 1, 1936. License shall cost One
Dollar ($1.00), fifteen cents (15c) to be retained by
the Circuit Court Clerk to defray expenses for ad
ministering the provisions of this Act, and five cents
(5c) shall be forwarded to the Department by the
Clerk for the use of the Department for printing of
necessary forms, inspections and expenses for ad
ministering the provisions of this Act, and the addi
tional eighty cents (80c) derived from the provi
sions of this Act shall be covered into the State
Treasury and credited to the general expenditure
fund.
Section Three (3). Persons exempt from license :
(a) No person shall be required to obtain an
operator's license for the purpose of driving or op
erating a road roller, road machinery, or any farm
tractor or implement of husbandry temporarily
drawn, moved or propelled on the highways.
(b) Every person in the service of the Army,
Navy or Marine Corps of the United States and
when furnished with a driver's permit and when
operating an official vehicle in such official service
shall be exempt from license under this Act.
Section Four (4). Non-residents, when exempt
from license:
(a) A non-resident over the age of sixteen years,
who has been duly licensed either as an operator
under a law requiring the licensing of operators in
his home state or country and who has in his imme
diate possession a valid operator's license issued
to him in his home state shall be permitted without
license under this Act to drive a motor vehicle upon
the highways of this State. Provided, however,
that this exemption shall not apply unless such state
accord licensed residents of Kentucky the same ex
emption.

138 Acts of the General Assembly
(b) A non-resident over the age of sixteen (16)
years whose home state does not require the licens
ing of operators and who has duly registered his
own motor vehicle for the current calendar year in
such home state shall be permitted to operate said
motor vehicle upon the highways of this state for
a period not longer than thirty (30) days in any one
year without making application for or obtaining
an operator's license under this Act. Provided,
however, that such non-resident may be required at
any time or place to prove lawful ownership, and
the right to operate such motor vehicle and estab
lish his identity.
Provided, however, that the Department reserves
the right at any time to refuse or discontinue the
exemptions set out in this section for any grounds
upon which a license issued under this Act may be
revoked or suspended.
(c) Any non-resident or other person whose
operator's license or right or privilege to operate
a motor vehicle in this State has been revoked or
suspended as provided in this Act shall not operate
a motor vehicle in this State under a license, permit
or registration certificate issued by any other juris
diction, or otherwise operate a motor vehicle in this
State during the period of such suspension, or
within one year after the date of such revocation.
Any person operating a motor vehicle in violation
of this section shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and
upon conviction shall be punished as provided in
Section 29 of this Act.
Section Five (5). What persons shall not be li
censed :
(a) An operator's license shall not be issued to
any person under the age of sixteen years.
(b) The Department shall not issue an opera
tor's license to any person whose license as operator

Chapter 13 139
has been suspended during the period for which
such license was suspended, nor to any person whose
license as operator has been revoked under the pro
visions of this Act, nor to any non-resident whose
privilege of exemption under Section 4 of this Act,
has been refused or discontinued, until the expira
tion of one year after such license was revoked.
(c) The Department shall not issue an opera
tor's license to any person who it has determined is
an habitual drunkard or is addicted to the use of
narcotic drugs.
(d) No operator's license shall be issued to any
applicant who has previously been adjudged insane
or an idiot, imbecile, epileptic, or feeble-minded, and
who has not at the time of such application been re
stored to competency by judicial decree or released
from a hospital for the insane or feeble-minded upon
a certificate of the superintendent that such person
is competent, nor then, unless, the Department is
satisfied that such person is competent to operate
a motor vehicle with safety to persons and property.
(e) The Department shall not issue an opera
tor's license to any person when in the opinion of
the Department such person is afflicted with or suf
fering from such physical or mental disability or
disease as will serve to prevent such person from
exercising reasonable and ordinary control over a
motor vehicle while operating the same upon the
highways, nor shall a license be issued to any per
son who is unable to understand highway warning
or direction signs in the English language.
(f) The Department shall not issue an opera
tor's license to any person who is required by this
Act to take an examination unless such person shall
have successfully passed such an examination.
(g) The Circuit Court Clerk shall not issue an
operator's license to any person who has suffered

140 Acts of the General Assembly
the loss of either an arm or limb, until such appli
cant has passed the examination as prescribed by
this Act, and the Clerk's recommendations for
warded to the Department and then only shall the
Clerk issue a license when so instructed by the De
partment.
Section Six (6). Age limits for drivers of school
buses and public passenger-carrying motor vehicles :
It shall be unlawful for any person, whether li
censed under this Act, or not, who is under the age
of twenty-one years, to drive a motor vehicle while
in use as a school bus for the transportation of
pupils to or from school, or to drive a motor vehicle
while in use as a public passenger-carrying vehicle.
Section Seven (7). Instruction permits and tem
porary licenses :
(a) Any person who, except for his lack of in
structions in operating a motor vehicle, would other
wise be qualified to obtain an operator's license
under this Act, may apply for a temporary license
and instruction permit, and the Circuit Court Clerk
shall issue such permit, entitling the applicant, while
having such permit in his possession, to drive a
motor vehicle, except a motorcycle, upon the high
ways for a period of thirty (30) days, but, except
when operating a motorcycle, such person must be
accompanied by a licensed operator or chauffeur
who is actually occupying a seat beside the driver.
Section Eight (8). Application for operator's
license or instruction permit:
(a) Every application for an instruction permit
or for an operator's license shall be made to the
Clerk of the Circuit Couit upon a form furnished by
the Department and shall be verified by the applica
tion before a person authorized to administer oaths.
(b) Every said application shall state the full
name, date of birth, sex, race, and residence ad

Chapter 13 141
dress of the applicant, and briefly describe the ap
plicant, and shall state whether the applicant has
heretofore been licensed as an operator, and, if so,
when and by what state or country, and whether
any such license has ever been suspended or revoked,
or whether an application has ever been refused,
and, if so, the date of and reason for such suspen
sion, revocation or refusal.
Section Nine (9). Application of minors:
The Department shall not grant the application
of any minor under the age of eighteen years for an
operator's license or instruction permit unless such
application is signed by the father of the applicant,
if the father is living, and has custody of the ap
plicant, otherwise by the mother or guardian having
the custody of such minor, or in the event the minor
under the age of eighteen years has no father,
mother or guardian, then an operator's license or
instruction permit shall not be granted to the minor
unless his application therefor is signed by his em
ployer.
Section Ten (10). Designation of Circuit Court
Clerk:
The Circuit Clerk of each county is hereby
authorized and designated to act for the Depart
ment for the purpose of issuing operators' licenses,
and keeping in his office at all times, available to the
Department, a record of all licenses issued in his
county, and all violations occuring within his county
under this Act. It shall be the duty of the Clerk to
forward a copy of the application received from a
person not residing in the county to which he has
applied for license to the Clerk of the county in
which applicant resides. It shall be the duty of the
Circuit Court Clerk to administer the oath required
under this Act to applicant without fee.

142 Acts of the General Assembly
Section Eleven (11). Register of operators:
(a) The Department shall file every application
for an operator's license numerically, and keep a
record of licenses which have been suspended or
revoked.
(b) In the case of a suspension or revocation of
a license by the Department, it shall immediately
transmit to the Circuit Court Clerk of the county
of the operator's residence notice of such suspen
sion or revocation and such other information as
may be necessary.
(c) The Circuit Court Clerk shall file every ap
plication for an operator's license and index the
same by name and also by number and maintain
suitable records of all licenses issued and all ap
plications for licenses denied, also a record of all
licenses which have been suspended or revoked, also
a record of all licenses reported to him as issued in
a foreign county, as well as a record of the suspen
sions and revocations thereof.
Section Twelve (12). Licenses issued to op
erators :
(a) If the Circuit Court Clerk shall determine
that the applicant, having fully complied with the
sections of this Act governing application and ex
amination, is entitled to an operator's license, he
shall issue same. Nothing in this Act shall prohibit
the applicant from appealing to the Department of
the Circuit Court Clerk refuses to issue license to
said applicant.
(b) Every such license shall bear thereon the
county in which issued and the distinguishing num
ber assigned to the license, and shall contain the
name, age, resident address and a brief description
of the licensee for the purpose of identification, also
a space for the signature of the licensee.

CHArTEB 13 143
Section Thirteen (13). Duplicate license certifi
cates:
In the event that an operator's license issued
under the provisions of this Act shall be lost or de
stroyed, the person to whom same was issued may
obtain a duplicate or substitute thereof by applying
in person and upon furnishing proof satisfactory
to the county issuing the original license that such
license has been lost or destroyed, and upon pay
ment of twenty-five cents (25c) to be retained by
the clerk.
Section Fourteen (14). License to be signed and
carried :
The licensee shall have such license in his im
mediate possession at all times when driving a
motor vehicle and shall display the same upon de
mand to the Circuit Court Clerk or examiner, to a
justice of the peace, a peace officer or a field deputy
or inspector of the Department. It shall be a de
fense to any charge under this subsection that the
person so charged produce in court an operator's
license, issued to such person before such arrest
and valid at the time of his arrest.
Section Fifteen (15). Expiration of licenses:
Every operator's license shall expire at midnight
July 31 each year. Every such license shall be re
newable on or before its expiration upon application
and payment of the required fee of one dollar ($1.00)
and shall be renewed without any examination un
less the Department has reason to believe that the
licensee is no longer qualified to receive a license.
Section Sixteen (16). Court to report convictions
and suspension of license :
Any Circuit Court having jurisdiction over
offenses committed under this Act or any other Act
of this state regulating the operation of motor

144 Acts of the General Assembly
vehicles on highways may suspend or revoke the
operator's license of the person so convicted, and
shall report the conviction, suspension, or revoca
tion to the Circuit Court Clerk who shall forward
such information to the Department, provided that
inferior courts may recommend to the Circuit Court
a suspension or revocation of any license issued
under this section.
Section Seventeen (17). Mandatory suspension
or revocation of licenses issued by the Department i
(a) It shall be the duty of the Circuit Court hav
ing jurisdiction to forthwith revoke the license of
any person convicted of any of the following crimes :
(1) Manslaughter resulting from the operation
of a motor vehicle. Assault and battery resulting
from the operation of a motor vehicle.
(2) Driving a vehicle while under the influence
of intoxicating liquor, or narcotic drug.
(3) Perjury or the making of a false affidavit to
the Department under this Act or any other law of
this State requiring the registration of motor ve
hicles or regulating their operation on highways.
(4) Any crime punishable as a felony under the
motor vehicle laws of this State or any other felony
in the commission of which a motor vehicle is used.
(5) Conviction or forfeiture of bail upon three
charges of reckless driving all within the preceding
twelve months.
(6) A conviction of a driver of a motor vehicle,
involved in an accident resulting in the death or
injury of another person upon a charge of failing
to stop and disclose his identity at the scene of the
accident.
(b) The Department upon receiving a record of
the conviction of any person upon a charge of op
erating a motor vehicle while the license of such
person is suspended or revoked, shall immediately

Chapter 13 145
extend the period of such first suspension or rev
ocation for an additional like period.
Section Eighteen (18). Discretionary suspension
or revocation by Court or Department:
(a) The Court or Department may immediately
suspend the license of any person with or without
hearing and with or without receiving a record of
conviction of such person of crime whenever the
Court or Department has reason to believe:
(1) That such person has committed any offenses
for the conviction of which mandatory revocation
of license is provided in Section 17.
(2) That such person has, by reckless or unlaw
ful operation of a motor vehicle, caused or con
tributed to an accident resulting in death or injury
or serious property damage.
(3) That such person is incompetent to drive
a motor vehicle or is afflicted with mental or physi
cal infirmities or disabilities rendering it unsafe
for such person to drive upon the highways.
(4) That such person is an habitual reckless or
negligent driver of a motor vehicle or has committed
a serious violation of the motor vehicle laws of this
State.
(b) The Department is hereby authorized to sus
pend or revoke the right of any non-resident to op
erate a motor vehicle in this State for any cause
for which the license of a resident operator may be
suspended or revoked, and any non-resident who
operates a motor vehicle upon a highway when his
right to operate has been suspended or revoked by
the Department shall be guilty of a misdemeanor
and subject to punishment as provided in Section 28.
(c) The Department is hereby authorized to sus
pend or revoke the license of any resident of this
State upon receiving notice of the conviction of
.such person in another state of an offense therein

146 Acts of the General Assembly
which if committed in this State, would be grounds
for the suspension or revocation of the license of an
operator. The Department is further authorized
upon receiving a record of the conviction in this
State of a non-resident driver of a motor vehicle of
any offense under the motor vehicle laws of this
State to forward a certified copy of such record to
the motor vehicle administrator in the state wherein
the person so convicted is a resident.
(d) The Department shall not suspend a license
for a period of more than one year and upon sus
pending or revoking any license shall require that
such license so suspended or revoked shall be sur
rendered to and retained by the Department except
that at the end of a period of suspension such license
so surrendered shall be returned to the licensee.
Section Nineteen (19). Right of appeal:
The hearing granted the licensee under Section 18
of this Act shall be held under the same legal pro
cedure and rights of parties as in criminal trials in
the county court.
Any person denied a license, or whose license has
been suspended and/or revoked by he Court or the
Department shall be granted an appeal from the
order of the Court or Department denying such
license, or from the order or judgment of the Court
or Department on hearing, to the circuit court of the
county in which the offense was committed, which
appeal and trial in the circuit court shall be gov
erned by the same procedure as on appeals from in
ferior courts to the circuit court, and the chief
agent or representative of the Department in said
county shall be the agent of the Department for the
service of all summons of other court process on all
hearings, appeals or trials under the provisions of
this Act. The licensee shall be granted a speedy
trial in the circuit court after due notice by sum

Chapter 13 147
inons, but by agreement of parties the trial may be
bold at any time after filing of appeal.
The licensee may further pray and shall be
granted an appeal from any final order or judgment
entered or rendered against him in said circuit
court, and the appeal and trial shall be the same as
other cases of the circuit court on appeal, except the
case may be heard before the circuit judge during
vacation by agreement of parties.
The circuit court is hereby vested with jurisdic
tion under the provisions of this Act. The licensee
shall be entitled to a change of venue in the circuit
court as provided by law.
Section Twenty (20). New license after revoca
tion :
Any person whose license is revoked under this
Act shall not be entitled to apply or receive any new
license until the expiration of one year from the
date such former license was revoked.
Section Twenty-One (21). When parent, guard
ian, or employer liable for negligence of minor:
Any negligence of a minor under the age of
eighteen years licensed upon application signed as
provided in section 9, when driving any motor ve
hicle upon a highway, shall be imputed to the person
who shall have signed the application of such minor
for said license, which person shall be jointly and
severably liable, with such minor, for any damages
by such negligence.
Section Twenty-Two (22). Owner liable for neg
ligence of minor:
Every owner of motor vehicle causing or know
ingly permitting a minor under the age of eighteen
years to drive such vehicle upon a highway, and
any person who gives or furnishes a motor vehicle
to such minor shall be jointly and severally liable

148 Acts of the General Assembly
with such minor for any damages caused by the
negligence of such minor in driving such vehicle.
Section Twenty-Two A (22A). The administra
tion of this act is vested in the Department which
may prescribe rules and regulations needed for the
enforcement of this Act.
Section Twenty-Three (23). Violation of license
provisions :
It shall be unlawful for any person to commit
any of the following acts :
(a) To display or cause to permit to be dis
played or to have in possession any operator's li
cense knowing the same to be fictitious or to have
been cancelled, revoked, suspended or altered;
(b) To lend to, or knowingly permit the use of
by one not entitled thereto, any operator's license
issued to the person so lending- or permitting the
use thereof;
(c) To display or to represent as one's own any
operator's license not issued to the person so dis
playing the same ;
(d) To fail to surrender to the Department upon
demand, any operator's license which has been sus
pended, cancelled or revoked as provided by law;
(e) To use a false or fictitious name or give a
false or fictitious address in any application for an
operator's license or any renewal or duplicate
thereof, or knowingly to make a false statement or
knowingly to conceal a material fact or otherwise
commit a fraud in any such application.
Section Twenty-Four (24). Making false affi
davit perjury.
Any person who shall make any false affidavit, or
shall knowingly swear or affirm falsely, to any mat
ter or thing required by the terms of this Act to be
sworn to or affirmed, shall be guilty of perjury and

Chapter 13 149
upon conviction shall be punished by fine or impris
onment as other persons committing perjury are
punishable.
Section Twenty-Five (25). Unlaivful to permit
violations of Act :
No person shall authorize or knowingly permit
a motor vehicle owned by him or under his control
to be driven by any person who has no legal right
to do so or in violation of any of the provisions of
this Act.
Section Twenty-Six (26). Unlawful to drive
while license suspended or revolted:
Any person whose operator's license has been
suspended or revoked, as provided in this Act, and
who shall drive any motor vehicle upon the high
ways of this State while such license is suspended
or revoked, shall be guilty of a misdemeanor, and
upon conviction shall be punished as provided in
Section 28 of this Act.
Section Twenty-Seven (27). Penalty for misde
meanor:
(a) It shall be a misdemeanor for any person to
violate any of the provisions of this Act unless such
violation is by this Act or other law of this State de
clared to be a felony.
(b) Unless another penalty is in this Act or by
the laws of this State provided, every person con
victed of a misdemeanor for the violation of any
provision of this Act shall be punished by a fine of
not less than twenty-five dollars ($25.00) nor more
than five hundred dollars ($500.00) or by imprison
ment for not more than six months, or by both such
fine and imprisonment.
Section Twenty-Eight (28). Penalty for driving
while license suspended or revoked:
Any person convicted of a violation of Section 26

150 Acts of the General Assembly
of this Act shall be punished by imprisonment in
the county jail for a period of not less than seven
(7) days nor more than six (6) months and there
may be imposed in addition thereto a fine of not
less than twenty-five ($25.00) nor more than five
hundred dollars ($500.00).
Section Twenty-Nine (29). Evidence of negli
gence in accident :
In addition to the penalties herein provided, if
any driver involved in any accident resulting in
any damage whatever either to person or to prop
erty shall be ineligible to procure operator's license
herein provided for or being eligible therefor shall
have failed to procure said operator's license, or
having procured same yet at the same time of such
accident same shall have been suspended or revoked,
he shall be deemed prima facie negligent in causing
or contributing to cause such accident.
Section Thirty (30). Short Title:
This Act may be cited as the "Uniform Oper
ator's License Act".
Section Thirty-One (31). Constitutionality.
If any part or parts of this Act shall be held to be
unconstitutional, such unconstitutionality shall not
affect the validity of the remaining parts of this
Act.
Section Thirty-Two (32). Repeal:
The existing operator's license, found in Chapter
eighteen (18) of the 1934 Extraordinary Session of
1he General Assembly now codified in Baldwin's
Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes, Sec
tions 2739m-1 to 2739m-32, inclusive, also Sections
2739g-15 to 2739g-21 inclusive in Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes, 1930 Edition, is hereby repealed, and all
acts or parts of acts inconsistent with the provi
sions of this Act are hereby repealed. Nothing in
this Act shall change or repeal the existing laws con

Chapter 14 151
cerning the licensing of drivers of busses or trucks
operating under the department or commissioner of
motor transportation.
Section Thirty-Three (33). Emergency.
Whereas, the present State revenue does not meet
the immediate needs of the maintenance of the State
government, its agencies and subdivisions, an emer
gency is declared to exist and this Act shall become
a law on August 1, 1936.
Approved by the Governor.
CHAPTER 14.
AN ACT concerning revenue and taxation and declaring an
emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One (1). Definitions.
(a) The word "person" as used in this Act in
cludes individuals, co-partnerships, business trusts,
joint stock companies, and corporations.
(b) The word "dealer" in this Act includes all
persons who are engaged in the sale of motor ve
hicles at retail.
(c) The term "Department" as used in this Act
shall mean the Department of Revenue of the Com
monwealth of Kentucky.
(d) "Retail price" shall be determined by the
price for which said vehicle with standard equip
ment sells in the county in which license is applied
for (or, if not sold in said county, then in the county
nearest said county in which said motor vehicle is
sold) by regularly authorized dealers in said motor
vehicles if a new car, and if a used car, by the price
or value given in the Automotive Reference Manual
used by the Department of Revenue.
(e) The word "motor vehicle" as used in this

152 Acts of the General Assembly
Act shall include all vehicles which are propelled
otherwise than by muscular power and which are
used for transportation of persons or property over
and upon the public highways of the Commonwealth,
excepting, however, road rollers and such vehicles
as travel exclusively on rails.
Section Two (2). There is hereby imposed on
the use in this Commonwealth of every motor ve
hicle, at the time it is first registered with the Coun
ty Clerk for the purpose of procuring licenses and
having license tags issued therefor, a tax equal to
three per cent on its retail price.
Section Three (3). There is expressly exempted
from the tax imposed by any of the provisions of
this Act :
(a) Cars sold to the United States, to this State,
to any political division of this State, to any munic
ipality, or to any charitable, religious, or educa
tional institution of the Commonwealth of Ken
tucky.
(b) Used cars registered or licensed in Ken
tucky during the same or immediately preceding
license period.
(c) Cars owned by a person bona fide domiciled
in another state and use and licensed in such state
by such owner during the same or immediately pre
ceding license period.
(d) Vehicles of a common carrier or contract
carrier upon which vehicle both a franchise and a
mileage tax is paid by the owner. And the owner
shall be required to exhibit both franchise and mile
age tax receipt for the same or immediately preced
ing license period.
Section Four (4). The tax hereby imposed shall
be collected by the County Clerk at the time he col
lects the registration fee for registering and licens
ing the said motor vehicle the first time such motor

Chapter 14
vehicle is offered for registration in the Common
wealth of Kentucky, and shall be accounted for by
him to the Department of Revenue as such registra
tion and license fees are by law accounted for, and
the said County Clerk shall be paid by the Common
wealth of Kentucky for his services in collecting
such tax, an amount equal to two per cent (2%) of
the tax so collected and accounted for.
Section Five (5). The County Clerk shall not
register or issue any license tags to the owner of
any motor vehicle affected by the provisions of this
Act, which motor vehicle is then being offered for
registration the first time, unless and until, in addi
tion to the registration fees and license fees imposed
by law, the owner shall pay the tax imposed herein.
Section Six (6). The Department is hereby
charged with the enforcement of this Act and may
promulgate such rules and regulations and pre
scribe such forms as are necessary or desirable for
the enforcement of its provisions. The Department,
or any representative appointed by it in writing,
is hereby authorized to examine the books, records,
papers, inventories, and files of any dealer pertain
ing to cars the use of which is made taxable by this
Act; to examine witnesses under oath for the pur
pose of verifying the accuracy of returns made;
and to examine the books, records, papers, and files
of dealers for the purpose of verifying reports made
under the provisions of this Act or of determining
whether or not the Act is being violated.
The Department shall have the power to institute
legal proceedings for the purpose of ascertaining
the amount of tax due under the provisions of this
Act and to enforce the collection thereof with the
penalties and interest thereon.
Section Seven (7). No suit shall be maintained
in any court to restrain or delay the collection of

154 Acts of the General Assembly
the tax herein imposed on any ground whatever, but
the aggrieved person shall pay under protest the
tax as and when due and may at any time within
two (2) years after the date of such payment sue
the Commonwealth through its agent, the Auditor
of Public Accounts, in an action in any court, State
or Federal, otherwise having jurisdiction of the
subject matter for the recovery of the tax so paid
with legal interest thereon from the date of pay
ment. If it can be finally determined that said tax
or any part thereof was wrongfully collected for
any reason, it shall be the duty of the Auditor of
Public Accounts to issue his warrant on the Treas
urer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the
amount of such tax so adjudged to have been wrong
fully collected, together with interest thereon; and
the Treasurer shall pay the same at once out of the
General Expenditure Fund of the State in prefer
ence to other warrants or claims against the Com
monwealth. Separate suits need not be filed for
each separate payment, but recovery may be had
in one suit for all suits heretofore paid or not
barred by the limitation of two (2) years herein im
posed.
Section Eight (8). All revenues derived from
the operation of this Act, shall as collected, be cov
ered into the State Treasury and credited to the
General Expenditure Fund.
Section Nine (9). Any person violating any of
the provisions of this Act shall be guilty of a mis
demeanor, and upon conviction, shall be fined not
less than twenty-five dollars ($25.00) nor more than
one thousand dollars ($1,000.00), and in case the
offender is an individual, may be imprisoned in the
county jail not exceeding six (6) months, or both
so fined and imprisoned.
Section Ten (10). Whereas, the present State

Chapter 15 155
revenue does not meet the immediate needs for the
maintenance of the State government and its agen
cies, an emergency is declared to exist and this Act
shall become a law and be effective on its passage
and approval by the Governor.
Approved by the Governor.
CHAPTER 15.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation and declaring an
emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Section One (1). Definitions, (a) "Places of
amusement and/or entertainment" shall mean and
include all theatres, ball parks and stadia, lecture
halls, dance halls, night clubs, skating rinks, public
bathing places, and all places of other sorts, whether
like the above mentioned or not, at which businesses
providing amusements and/or entertainments for
which a charge is made, are operated, but shall not
mean nor include race tracks subject to the super
vision of the Kentucky Pacing Commission.
(b) The term "sale" shall mean and include the
transfer or exchange for valuable consideration of
services of all classes and kinds.
(c) The word "person" shall mean and include
individuals, firms, joint stock companies, syndicates
and corporations.
(d) "Professional entertainers" shall mean and
include any person or persons who receive pay in
money or money's worth for their performances.
(e) "Proprietor" shall mean and include any
person who owns, operates, or manages a place of
amusement and/or entertainment.
(f) "Department" shall mean and include the
Department of Revenue of Kentucky.
Acta — 6

156 Acts of the General Assemble
Section Two (2). An excise tax is hereby im
posed on the sale of admission to places of amuse
ment and/or entertainment at the following rate:
If the admission charge is eleven cents (11c) to
eighteen cents (18c) inclusive, the rate shall be one
cent (1c) ; if the admission charge is nineteen cents
(19c) to twenty-eight cents (28c) inclusive, the rate
shall be two cents (2c) ; if the admission charge is
twenty-nine cents (29c) to thirty-eight cents (38c)
inclusive the rate shall be three cents (3c) ; if the
rate is in excess of thirty-eight cents (38c) the
rate shall be three cents (3c) plus one cent (lc)
additional for each five cents (5c) or fractional part
thereof. Provided that admission charges of less
than eleven cents (He) shall not be subject to tax
hereunder.
In the case of places of amusement and/or enter
tainment at which food or drink is served and pro
fessional entertainers are employed, the charge for
admission shall be deemed to be twenty-five per
cent (25%) of the total charge made or the amount
of the cover charge, if any, whichever is greater.
In the case of all amusements and/or entertain
ments, the charge for admission shall be deemed to
be the total charge imposed and if there is a charge
for seating, or for special facilities within the place
of amusement and/or entertainment, the charge for
admission shall be deemed to include both the
charge for general admission and the charge for
the special seating or other facilities provided in
the places of amusement and/or entertainment.
Nothing in this Act shall be construed to impose
a tax upon admissions to athletic contests, plays,
and other forms of amusement and/or entertain
ment, at least seventy-five per cent (75%) of the
gross proceeds of which admissions are to be used

Chapter 15 157
exclusively for charitable, religious, and/or educa
tional purposes within this State.
(a) The proprietor of each place of amusement
and/or entertainment subject to the provisions of
this Act shall on or before the tenth (10) day of
each month make return to the Department on such
forms as it may prescribe, setting out the total
gross receipts from the place of business, the amount
thereof which has been paid as a separate charge
for admission, the amount thereof which has been
paid for food and/or drink, the number of admis
sions of each price class which have been sold by
the said place of amusement and/or entertainment
and such additional information as the Department
may require and shall accompany such return with
a certified or cashier's check for the amount of tax
due hereunder.
(b) Every person vending theater or other ad
mission tickets to places of amusement and/or enter
tainment whose business is subject to tax hereunder
shall be required before engaging in such business
to procure from the Department of Revenue a per
mit to engage in such business, and the Department
may condition such permit on continual conformity
with this Act and with the rules and regulations
issued hereunder.
Every place of amusement and/or entertainment,
taxable under this Act, not heretofore specifically
required under the statutes of the Commonwealth
to be licensed shall procure from the Department
of Revenue a permit before engaging in any of the
businesses made taxable hereunder.
To assure conformity with the provisions of this
Act, the Department is hereby authorized and re
quired to allot to each place of amusement and/or
entertainment using admission tickets a serial num

158 Acts of the General Assembly
ber or serial numbers by means of which the De
partment may check on the number and price class
of admissions to all such places of amusement
and/or entertainment. The supervision by the De
partment to enforce the provisions hereof are hereby
authorized to be made effective through control over
the vending of tickets to said places of amsuement
and/or entertainment.
Provided, however, that nothing in this Act shall
be construed as placing a tax upon admissions to
athletic contests, unless the price charged for said
admissions to said athletic contests shall exceed the
sum of fifty cents (50c) ; in which case the first fifty
cents (50c) of such admission shall be exempt from
the tax levied under this Act.
Section Three (3). (a) The administration of
this Act is vested in the Department of Revenue
which may prescribe forms and promulgate rules
and regulations needed for the enforcement of the
Act. The Department or any representative ap
pointed by it in writing, is hereby authorized to ex
amine the books, records, papers, files, equipment
and/ or inventory of any person or persons pertain
ing to services made taxable by this Act, to examine
witnesses under oath for the purpose of verifying
returns made by persons or for other purposes in
volving enforcement of this Act, and to examine the
books, records, papers, files, equipment and/or in
ventory of other persons for the purpose of deter
mining whether the Act is being violated.
(b) The Department shall have power to insti
tute legal proceedings for the purpose of ascertain
ing the amount of tax due and of enforcing the col
lection thereof with penalties thereon and for the
purpose of enjoining the operation of the business
of the proprietor until said tax is paid.

Chapter 15 159
(c) The Department may at or after the com
mencement of an action for the purpose of collecting
the amount of tax due and penalty and interest
thereon as set out herein, have an attachment
against the property of the proprietor or granish-
ment of his debtors, without the execution of bond,
upon filing an affidavit showing the nature of the
claim, that it is just, the sum which the Department
believes the State should recover from the pro
prietor, and any of the grounds upon which an at
tachment is authorized by the civil code of practice
in civil causes.
(d) Any information obtained by the Depart
ment or any agent or representative thereof, as a
result of the reports, investigations or verifications
herein authorized and required to be made, shall be
confidential as respecting the business of any per
son, and any person divulging such information, ex
cept on the order of a court of competent jurisdic
tion, or to an officer of the Commonwealth entitled
to receive the same in his official capacity, shall be
fined not exceeding five hundred dollars ($500.00),
provided, however, that this provision shall not pre
vent the Department from publishing statistics of
tax receipts and volumes of sales in such a manner
as not to reveal information concerning the business
of any individual.
(e) Every place of amusement and/or entertain
ment whose business is taxable under the terms of
this Act is hereby required to make bond conditioned
on the faithful compliance with the terms of this
Act with a surety satisfactory to the Department
of Revenue. In the event the department believes
that administration can be rendered more effective
or in the event the owner of the place of amusement
and/or entertainment is unwilling or unable to make
bond, the Department is hereby authorized to cause

160 Acts of the General Assembly
to be prepared adhesive stamps and/or officially
stamped admission tickets which shall be sold to
owners of places of amusement and/or entertain
ment in advance, the said owner to pay the Depart
ment the tax value of such tickets plus the costs of
preparing and delivering to the said owner the said
adhesive stamps and/or officially stamped admis
sion tickets.
(f ) The Department shall keep a suitable record
of the sale of all officially stamped admission tickets
which shall show the dates of sale thereof and the
names of the purchasers.
Section Four (4). (a) The officially stamped ad
mission tickets shall serve as stamps for the pur
pose of evidencing payment of the tax herein im
posed on admissions to places of amusement and/or
entertainment, and shall indicate on the face the-
charge for admission. These tickets shall be sold
by the Department to persons operating places of
amusement and/or entertainment; and the Depart
ment shall prescribe suitable regulations respecting
the manner in which said tickets shall be used in
order to conform to the provisions of this Act.
(b) No person operating a pace of amusement
and/or entertainment except a person bonded under
the provisions hereof shall use any admission tickets
except such as are distributed by the Department;
provided, however, that in the case of season tickets
and in other cases in which it is impractical for the
Department to require the use of such admission
tickets, the person operating the place of amuse
ment and/or entertainment shall place adhesive
stamps evidencing the payment of the tax on the said
admission ticket. These stamps shall be firmly at
tached to the tickets in such manner that they can
not be readily removed therefrom.
(c) Except as otherwise provided by the regula-

Chapter 15 161
tions promulgated by the Department for the en
forcement of this Act, as each individual is admitted
to places of amusement and/or entertainment, his
ticket shall be collected and immediately torn in
two parts, approximately through the middle, and
one-half given to the patron, the other half being
retained. If adhesive stamps are used, as herein
provided, the ticket shall be torn in such way as to
break the stamp in two parts.
Section Five (5). Whoever removes, washes, re
stores, alters, or otherwise prepares an adhesive
stamp or officially stamped admission ticket with
intent to use or cause to be used, after it had already
been once used, or whoever knowingly or willfully
buys, sells, offers for sale, or gives away such
washed, restored, or altered stamp or officially
stamped admission ticket, to any person, or know
ingly uses the same, or has in his possesion any
washed, restored, or altered stamps which have been
removed from admission tickets, to which they have
previously been affixed, or whoever, for the purpose
of indicating the payment of any tax herein im
posed, uses again any stamp or officially stamped
admission ticket that has theretofore been used, for
the purpose of denoting the payment of the tax pro
vided in this Act, or whoever prepares, buys, sells
or offers for sale, or knowingly has in his posses
sion any counterfeit stamps, is guilty of a felony
and, upon conviction, shall be punished by a fine of
not more than one thousand dollars ($1,000.00) or
by imprisonment for not more than five (5) years,
or by both such fine and imprisonment. Provided,
however, that if such violator be other than a nat
ural person, then the principal officer and/or the
officer or officers responsible for such violation may
be so imprisoned.
Section Six (6). Any person required by this

162 Acts of the General Assembly
Act to affix the stamps or use officially stamped ad
mission tickets as provided in this Act, who shall
fail properly to affix the stamps or officially stamped
admission tickets, within the time prescribed by
law, or who shall fail to pay the tax imposed in
Section 2, subsection (b) of this Act, shall be re
quired to pay as a part of the tax imposed here
under a penalty of one hundred per cent (100%) of
the -amount of tax due to be assessed and collected
by the Department as other taxes herein imposed
are collected. Any admission tickets in the place
of business of any person required by the provisions
of this Act to stamp the same shall be prima facie
evidence that such articles are intended for sale or
use in this State.
Section Seven (7). No person shall sell, give, or
lend any stamps, nor shall any person purchase,
accept, receive, or borrow any stamps from any per
son; it being the intent hereof, in order to prevent
fraud and insure collection of the taxes due, to limit
all sales and transfers of such stamps to the Depart
ment or its authorized representative.
Section Eight (8). It is the intent and purpose
of this Act to impose the tax levied herein once only
upon the sales of the same services made taxable
hereunder.
Section Nine (9). No suit shall be maintained in
any court to restrain or delay the collection or pay
ment of the tax levied by this Act. The aggrieved
taxpayer shall pay under protest the tax as and
when required and may at any time within two
(2) years from the date of such payment sue the
Commonwealth through its agent, the Auditor of
Public Accounts, in an action at law in any court,
state or federal, otherwise having jurisdiction of
the parties and subject matter, for the recovery of
the tax paid with legal interest thereon from the

Chapteb 15 163
date of payment. If it is finally determined that
said tax or any part thereof was wrongfully col
lected, for any reason, it shall be the duty of the
Auditor of Public Accounts to issue his warrant on
the Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
for the amount of such tax so adjudged to have been
wrongfully collected together with legal interest
thereon. The Treasurer shall pay the same at once
out of the general expenditure fund of the State in
preference to other warrants or claims against the
Commonwealth. A separate suit need not be filed
for each individual payment made by any taxpayer,
but a recovery may be had in one suit for as many
payments as may have been made.
Section Ten (10). If services, the sale of which
is made taxable by this Act, are distributed under
such circumstances that the Constitution and laws
of the United States or of Kentucky preclude the
possibilities of imposing the tax, then such tax shall
not apply.
Section Eleven (11). Any person who shall vio
late any of the provisions of this Act not hereby
made a felony shall be deemed guilty of a misde
meanor and upon conviction shall be punished by a
fine of not more than five hundred dollars ($500.00)
for each offense and said person's license, if any be
required by law, shall be subject to revocation at
the discretion of the Court.
Section Twelve (12). All revenue derived from
the operation of this Act as collected and recorded
shall be deposited through the Department in the
State Treasury and credited to the General Ex
penditure Fund.
Section Thirteen (13). The provisions of this
Act are severable. If any provision, section, or
paragraph thereof, or if the application of any pro

164 Acts of the General Assembly
vision, section or paragraph to any class of sales
or uses affected by the operation of the Act, should
be held unconstitutional or invalid, such decision re
garding the provision, section, paragraph, or appli
cation shall not affect or impair the remainder of
the Act, it being hereby declared the legislative in
tent to tax separately the sale of each class of serv
ice contemplated by any of its provisions.
Section Fourteen (14). Emergency. Whereas the
present State revenue does not meet the immediate
needs for the maintenance of the State government,
its agencies and subdivisions, an emergency is de
clared to exist and this Act shall become a law and
be effective upon its passage and approval by the
Governor.
Be it provided, however, that pending prepara
tion of stamps and completion of other administra
tive plans, the effective date of this Act shall be one
o'clock A. M. of the date three (3) calendar weeks
after this measure is approved by the Governor.
Approved by the Governor.
CHAPTER 16.
AN ACT to repeal, amend and re-enact Chapter one hundred
fifty-one (151) of the Acts of the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky of 1932 now codified as Section
4238 of Baldwin's 1933 Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes, "being an Act relating to revenue and taxation so
as to increase the state tax on the recording of certain in
struments and marriage licenses", and declaring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
Section One (1). That Chapter one hundred fifty-
one (151) of the Acts of the General Assembly of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky of 1932 now codified
as Section 4238 of Baldwin's 1933 Supplement to

Chapter 16 165
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes, "being an Act relating
to revenue and taxation so as to increase the State
tax on the recording of certain instruments and
marriage licenses" be and the same is hereby re
pealed, amended and re-enacted so that said section
as amended and re-enacted shall read:
(a) There is imposed a tax of four dollars ($4.00)
on each verdict of a petit jury in civil cases in
circuit court;
(b) There is imposed a tax of three dollars and
fifty cents ($3.50) on each marriage license ;
(c) There is imposed upon the filing and regis
tering of a plat and certificate of survey, and issuing
and recording patent thereon one charge which
shall be paid before the survey is filed, but not to
apply to a survey heretofore filed in the register's
office, a tax of two dollars and fifty cents ($2.50)
for each survey consisting of ten (10) acres or less,
and a tax of five dollars ($5.00) for each survey in
excess of ten (10) acres;
(d) There is imposed a tax of two dollars ($2.00)
on each appeal to the Court of Appeals;
(e) There is imposed a tax of one dollar ($1.00)
on each deed or power of attorney to convey real
or personal estate ; on each mortgage or conveyance
of personal property; on each lien or conveyance
of coal, oil, gas or other mineral right or privilege ;
but no tax shall be paid on any chattel mortgage
unless the amount thereof exceeds two hundred
($200.00) dollars;
(f ) There is imposed a tax of one dollar ($1.00)
for issuing a land warrant, for each official certifi
cate with seal of office affixed;
(g) There is imposed a tax of fifty cents (50c)
as follows : On each original action or proceeding
in the circuit court, commencing with or without
original process, and on each original action or pro

166 Acts of the General Assembly
ceeding in other courts, when the amount in con
troversy, exclusive of interest and costs, exceeds
fifty dollars ($50.00) ; and on each appeal to the cir
cuit or quarterly court and on each traverse or
forcible entry or detainer and on each proceeding
to reverse a judgment;
(h) There is imposed a tax of fifty cents (50c)
on the seal of court required by law to keep a seal
except for the purpose of issuing the license author
ized by the State ;
(i) There is imposed a tax of fifty cents (50c)
for a copy of a plat; for a copy of a course and
distances of any patent or survey; and for a copy
of an entry or land warrant;
(j) There is imposed a tax of twenty-five cents
(25c) for filing a caveat; for filing a copy of judg
ment of a court in a case of a caveat, mandamus or
other proceeding affecting the land office, or the
duties of the register; and for a copy of an assign
ment;
(k) There is imposed a tax of two cents (2c) for
each twenty words of : a copy of a caveat ; a copy
of any writing not specifically provided for.
Said tax shall be due and payable when said proc
esses or instruments are filed or issued, or copies
thereof furnished.
Section Two (2). All revenues derived under the
provisions of this Act shall be covered into the State
Treasury and credited to the General Expenditure
Fund.
Section Three (3). Whereas, the present State
revenue does not meet the immediate needs for the
maintenance of the State government and its
agencies an emergency is declared to exist and this
Act shall become a law and be effective on its passage
and approval by the Governor.
Approved by the Governor.

Chapter 17 167
.- CHAPTER 17.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation of the gross receipts
of utilities and declaring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
Section One (1). Definitions :
(a) The word "person" as used in this Act in
cludes individuals, co-partnerships, business trusts,
joint stock companies, and corporations.
(b) The term "gross receipts" as used in this
Act shall be construed to mean the amount received
in money, credits, property, or other money's worth,
regardless of whether or not the form thereof be
enumerated herein. Provided, that in case the busi
ness is conducted on credit the consideration shall
be deemed to be gross receipts when payment shall
be made in cash, property, or other money's worth,
regardless of whether or not the form be numerated
herein.
(c) The term "taxpayer" as used in this Act in
cludes all persons required hereunder to make re
ports and pay the tax or taxes imposed by this Act.
(d) The term "Department" as used in this
Act shall mean the Department of Revenue of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky.
(e) The word "sale" as used in this Act in
cludes the transfer of services for valuable consid
eration as well as similar transfers of property.
(f) The word "gas" as used in this Act shall
mean the substance . or substances commercially
known as "illuminating gas."
Section Two (2). An excise tax is hereby im
posed on the sale of electric current, water, artifi
cial, natural or mixed gas, and on furnishing the
ways or means for the transmission of the voice or
messages other than those transmitted by United

168 Acts of the General Assembly
States Mail from one person in the Commonwealth
to another person in the Commonwealth, equal to
three per cent (3%) of the aggregate gross receipts
of such persons so engaged in such respective busi
nesses; and every such person is required to col
lect the tax from the purchaser and account to the
State of Kentucky for such tax in the manner pro
vided in this Act. Provided, however, that no tax
shall be imposed upon the transmission of the voice
and/or messages for, or upon sales of electric cur
rent, water, artificial, natural or mixed gas to, the
United States, this State, any Kentucky municipal
ity, any political subdivision of this State, or any
charitable, religious, or educational institution in
Kentucky; and provided, further, that no tax is by
this section imposed upon sales of electric current,
water, artificial, natural or mixed gas for the pur
pose of resale, nor upon the sale of electric current
or artificial or natural gas for use in manufactur
ing, processing, mining or refining. Sales of elec
tric current, water, artificial, natural or mixed gas,
in interstate commerce are specifically excluded
from the provisions of this Act.
Section Three (3). The taxpayer is hereby re
quired to keep and preserve such written and other
records as the Department may require to facili
tate enforcement of this Act. On or before the ex
piration of fifteen (15) days following the end of
each month, beginning with the month after which
this Act becomes effective, the taxpayer shall mail
to or file with the Department a verified written re
port of the total gross receipts made during the
preceding month, together with such additional in
formation as the Department may require for en
forcement of this Act, and shall remit with said re
port the amount of tax due under Section 2 of this
Act. Provided, however, that the Department, on

Chapter 17 169
the basis of information herein authorized to be se
cured or on the basis of an audit of the return, may
require additional payments if found to be due.
Section Four (4). The Department is hereby
charged with the enforcement of this Act and may
promulgate such rules and regulations and pre
scribe such forms as are necessary or desirable for
the enforcement of its provisions. The Depart
ment, or any representative appointed by it in writ
ing, is hereby authorized to examine the books, rec
ords, papers, files, equipment and/or meters of any
taxpayer pertaining to the gross receipts made tax
able by this Act; to examine witnesses under oath
for the purpose of verifying the accuracy of returns
made by taxpayers; and to examine the books, rec
ords, papers, files, equipment and/or meters of
manufacturers, wholesale dealers, or carriers, in
cluding pipe line companies, for the purpose of veri
fying reports made under the provisions of this Act
or of determining whether or not the Act is being
violated.
The Department shall have the power to institute
legal proceedings for the purpose of ascertaining
the amount of tax due under the provisions of this
Act and to enforce the collection thereof with the
penalties thereon. The Department may at or after
the commencement of an action for the purpose of
collecting the amount of tax due and penalties and
interest thereon, as set out herein, have an attach
ment against the property of the taxpayer or gar
nishment against his creditors without the execu
tion of a bond upon the filing of an affidavit alleging
the nature of the claim, that it is just, the sum which
the Department believes the State should recover
from the taxpayer, or any of the grounds upon
which the Civil Code of Practice authorizes the
issuance of an attachment in civil causes.

170 Acts of the General Assembly
Section Five (5). Any information obtained by
the Department or any agent or representative
thereof, as a result of the reports, investigations, or
verifications herein authorized to be made, shall be
confidential and any person divulging any such in
formation, except upon order of a court of compe
tent jurisdiction or to an officer of the Common
wealth entitled to receive the same in his official
capacity, shall be fined not exceeding Five Hundred
Dollars ($500.00).
Section Six (6). No suit shall be maintained in
any court to restrain or delay the collection of the
tax herein imposed on any ground whatever, but the
aggrieved taxpayer shall pay under protest the tax
as and when required and may at any time within
two (2) years after date of such payment sue the
Commonwealth through its agent, the Auditor of
Public Accounts, in an action in any court, State or
Federal, otherwise having jurisdiction of the sub
ject matter for the recovery of the tax so paid with
legal interest thereon from the date of payment. If
it be finally determined that said tax or any part
thereof was wrongfully collected for any reason it
shall be the duty of the Auditor of Public Accounts
to issue his warrant on the Treasurer of the Com
monwealth of Kentucky for the amount of such tax
so adjudged to have been wrongfully collected, to
gether with interest thereon; and the Treasurer
shall pay the same at once out of the General Ex
penditure Fund of the State in preference to other
warrants or claims against the Commonwealth.
Seperate suits need not be filed for each separate
payment by any taxpayer, but recovery may be had
in one suit for all amounts theretofore paid and not
barred by the limitation of two (2) years herein
imposed.
Section Seven (7). Any taxpayer who shall fail

Chapter 17 171
or refuse to make the returns herein prescribed or
shall refuse to permit the Department, its agents,
or representatives appointed by it in writing, to
examine books, records, papers, files, equipment
and/or meters of such persons pertaining to the
gross receipts made taxable by this Act, or who
makes an incomplete, false, or fraudulent return
hereunder, or who does or attempts to do anything
whatever to avoid full disclosure of the amount of
business done or to avoid the payment of the whole
or any part of the tax or penalties due, shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor and upon conviction shall
be fined not exceeding one thousand dollars ($1,000)
and, in case the offender is an individual, may be
imprisoned in the county jail not exceeding six (6)
months or both so fined and imprisoned, provided
that in case such violator be a business trust, joint
stock company, corporation or fiduciary, the princi
pal officer and/or the officer or officers responsible
for such violation may be punished by such impris
onment. Such a fine shall be additional to other
fines and/or penalties herein provided. Any tax
payer who shall fail to pay the tax due hereunder in
the time specified in this Act shall pay a penalty
equal to twenty per cent (20%) of the amount of
tax due and delinquent and in addition thereto in
terest at the rate of six per cent (6%) per annum
on both the original amount of tax and the penalty
imposed in this paragraph.
Section Eight (8). All revenues derived from
the operation of this Act shall, as collected, be cov
ered into the State Treasury and credited to the
General Expenditure Fund.
Section Nine (9). Whereas, the present State
revenue does not meet the immediate needs for the
maintenance of the State government and its agen
cies, an emergency is declared to exist ; and this Act

172 Acts of the General Assembly
shall become a law and be effective on its passage
and approval by the Governor.
Approved May 12, 1936.
CHAPTER 18.
A JOINT AND COCURRENT RESOLUTION providing for the
payment of money to Mrs. Dora Evans, Mother of C. E. Evans,
deceased member of the House of Representatives of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky.
WHEREAS, the Honorable C. E. Evans was a duly elected and
qualified member of the General Assembly of the Common
wealth of Kentucky for the full term of the General Assembly
beginning in January, one thousand nine hundred thirty-six,
for a period of two years, and
WHEREAS, the said C. E. Evans has been unable to attend
this session since April 5, 1936, because of death, and
WHEREAS, this being an extraordinary session of the General
Assembly during the term of the said C. E. Evans, and
WHEREAS, the Mother of the said C. E. Evans, having been
dependent upon him would have received remuneration bene
fits had he been able to attend: now, therefore,
Be it Resolved by the House of Representatives of
Kentucky, the Senate concurring therein;
That Mrs. Dora Evans, the Mother of the said de
ceased member of the House of Representatives of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky, be paid his per
diem of $10.00 as a member of the House of Repre
sentatives for the entire special session of one thou
sand nine hundred thirty-six, as though the said
C. E. Evans had been personally present at each of
said sessions during said special session, and
That the Clerk of the House of Representatives
is hereby directed to issue his respective order on
the Auditor of Public Accounts of the Common
wealth of Kentucky for the said sum that will be
due when the total number of legislative days of
the special session shall have been determined, the
said orders to be payable to Mrs, Dora Evans,

Chapter 19 173
Mother of the deceased member, C. E. Evans, and
the Auditor of Public Accounts shall issue his war
rant on the Treasurer of the Commonwealth for the
amount thereof.
Approved by the Governor.
CHAPTER 19.
WHEREAS, a Resolution introduced by Mr. Clay, of Henderson
County, adopted during the Regular Session of the General
Assembly, 1936, directed the State Librarian to secure eight
copies of the 1936 Supplement of the Carroll's Kentucky Stat
utes for the use of the members of the House, and,
WHEREAS, a Resolution offered by Senator Rives, was adopted
in the Senate on January 8, 1936, authorizing the State
Librarian to secure three copies of the 1930 Kentucky Statutes,
eight copies of the 1933 Supplement, eight copies of all Acts
of the General Assembly since 1933, and one copy of Kentucky
Code of Practice, and,
WHEREAS, statement has been presented the Chief Clerk of
the House for Eighty Dollars ($80.00) to pay for books pur
chased for the House, and,
WHEREAS, statement has been presented the Chief Clerk of
the Senate for Two Hundred Dollars ($200.00) to pay for
books purchased for the use of the Senate, and,
WHEREAS, the Auditor of Public Accounts has refused to
issue his warrant to pay these bills because the Resolutions
do not state from what fund the money is to be paid; there
fore,
Be it Resolved by the House of Representatives,
the Senate concurring therein:
That the Chief Clerk of the respective Houses,
shall certify to the Auditor of Public Accounts a
copy of this Resolution and a statement of the bills
rendered him for aforesaid mentioned books, and
that the Auditor be directed to draw his warrant
for same and charge it to Legislative Expense Fund.
Whereas, this account has been long past due, and
payment should not be deferred any longer than

174 Acts of the General Assembly
necessary, an emergency is hereby declared to exist
and this Resolution shall take effect from and after
its passage and approval by the Governor.
Neither approved nor disapproved.
